---
title: linear space
---
## 函数空间
[math formula: X \subseteq \mathbb{C}:= F, V = \{f: X \to \mathbb{C}\}]
- 在[math formula: V]上定义加法
  [math formula: 
  (f+g)(x) = f(x) + g(x)
  ]
易得[math formula: \langle V, + \rangle]是一个交换群
- 在[math formula: V]上定义数乘
  [math formula: 
  (\lambda f)(x) = \lambda f(x)
  ]
易得
- [math formula: (a + b)f = af + bf]
- [math formula: a(f + g) = af + ag]
- [math formula: (ab)f = a(bf)]
- [math formula: 1f = f]
所以[math formula: \langle F, V \rangle]是一个线性空间
## k阶可导的函数空间
[math formula: X \subseteq \mathbb{R}, U_k = \{f: X \to \mathbb{R} \mid f \text{ is differentiable at least k times}\}]
易得[math formula: U_k]是[math formula: V]的子空间
## 函数空间上的求导(算子)
[math formula: D: U_k \to U_{k-1}, (Df)(x) = f'(x)]
有
- [math formula: D(f+g) = Df + Dg]
- [math formula: D(\lambda f) = \lambda Df]
所以求导算子[math formula: D]是线性映射
线性映射复合线性映射仍是线性映射
因此
[math formula: 
D^k = D \circ D \circ \cdots \circ D
]
是线性映射
线性映射的线性组合仍是线性映射,因此
[math formula: 
\sum_{i=0}^k \lambda_i D^i
]
是线性映射
## 多元函数
[math formula: X \subseteq \mathbb{C}^n, V = \{f: X \to \mathbb{C}\}]
- 在[math formula: V]上定义加法
  [math formula: 
  (f+g)(x) = f(x) + g(x)
  ]
易得[math formula: \langle V, + \rangle]是一个交换群
- 在[math formula: V]上定义数乘
  [math formula: 
  (\lambda f)(x) = \lambda f(x)
  ]
易得
- [math formula: (a + b)f = af + bf]
- [math formula: a(f + g) = af + ag]
- [math formula: (ab)f = a(bf)]
- [math formula: 1f = f]
所以[math formula: \langle F, V \rangle]是一个线性空间

78.content:
---
title: SDE Diffusion
---
- Score-based Generative Model through SDE
	- 1. Notations
	- 2. Score-based Diffusion through SDE (Unconditional)
	- 3. Training (Unconditional)
	- 4. Score-based Diffusion through SDE (Conditional)
	- 5. Special Case
	- 6. Example: Continuous DDPM (Unconditional)
# Score-based Generative Model through SDE
## 1. Notations
The meaning of [math formula:  \nabla \cdot [\cdot] ]:
For scalar function [math formula:  \phi(\mathbf{x}): \mathbb{R}^d \to \mathbb{R} ], we know [math formula:  \nabla \phi(\mathbf{x}) ] is the gradient.
For vector function [math formula:  \phi(\mathbf{x}): \mathbb{R}^d \to \mathbb{R}^d ], [math formula: \nabla \cdot \phi(\mathbf{x}) \in \mathbb{R} ] is the divergence, defined as the sum of the partial derivatives of each component:
[math formula: 
\nabla \cdot \phi(\mathbf{x}) = \sum_{i=1}^{d} \frac{\partial \phi_i}{\partial x_i}
]
For matrix function [math formula: \phi(\mathbf{x}): \mathbb{R}^d \to \mathbb{R}^{d \times d} ], its divergence is a vector, defined as:
[math formula: 
\nabla \cdot \phi(\mathbf{x}) = \left( \sum_{j=1}^{d} \frac{\partial \phi_{1j}}{\partial x_j}, \ldots, \sum_{j=1}^{d} \frac{\partial \phi_{dj}}{\partial x_j} \right)^\top
]
or written in a more general form:
[math formula: 
\nabla \cdot \phi(\mathbf{x}) := \left( \nabla \cdot \phi^1(\mathbf{x}), \ldots, \nabla \cdot \phi^d(\mathbf{x}) \right)^\top
]
where [math formula:  \phi^i(\mathbf{x}) ] is the [math formula:  i ]th row of [math formula:  \phi ].
## 2. Score-based Diffusion through SDE (Unconditional)
SDE of forward process(noise):
[math formula: 
dx = f(x, t) dt + g(x, t) dw\\
f: \mathbb{R}^d \times [0, T] \to \mathbb{R}^d, g: \mathbb{R}^d \times [0, T] \to \mathbb{R}^{d \times d}
]
SDE of reverse process(denoise):
[math formula: 
dx = \left(f(x, t) - \nabla \cdot [g(x, t)g(x, t)^T]  -  g(x, t)g(x, t)^T \nabla_x \ln p_t (x)\right) dt + g(x, t) dw
]
probability flow ODE:
[math formula: 
dx = \left(f(x, t) - \frac{1}{2} \nabla \cdot [g(x, t)g(x, t)^T]  -  \frac{1}{2} g(x, t)g(x, t)^T \nabla_x \ln p_t (x)\right) dt
]
## 3. Training (Unconditional)
- Sliced Score Matching:
[math formula: 
\theta^* = \arg \min_\theta \mathbb{E}_t \left\{ \lambda(t) \mathbb{E}_{\mathbf{x}(0)} \mathbb{E}_{\mathbf{x}(t)} \mathbb{E}_{\mathbf{v} \sim p_{\mathbf{v}}} \left[ \mathbf{v}^\top \mathbf{s}_\theta(\mathbf{x}(t), t) \mathbf{v} +  \frac{1}{2} (\mathbf{v}^T \mathbf{s}_\theta(\mathbf{x}(t), t) )^2 \right] \right\}\\
]
- Denoising Score Matching:
  [math formula: 
  \theta^* = \arg \min_\theta \mathbb{E}_{t \sim U(0, T)} \lambda(t) \mathbb{E}_{x_0 \sim p_0} \left[ \mathbb{E}_{x \sim p_{0t}} \left[ \left\| s_\theta (x,t) - \nabla_x \ln p_{0t} (x|x_0) \right\|_2^2 \right] \right]
  ]
## 4. Score-based Diffusion through SDE (Conditional)
[math formula: 
dx = \left(f(x, t) - \nabla \cdot [g(x, t)g(x, t)^T]  -  g(x, t)g(x, t)^T \nabla_x \ln p_t (x|y)\right) dt + g(x, t) dw
]
[math formula: 
\nabla_x \ln p_t (x|y) = \nabla_x \ln \left(\frac{p_t (y|x)p_t (x)}{p_t (y)}\right) = \nabla_x \ln p_t (y|x) + \nabla_x \ln p_t (x)
]
[math formula: 
dx = \left(f(x,t) -  \nabla \cdot [g(x, t)g(x, t)^T]  -  g(x, t)g(x, t)^T \left(\nabla_x \ln p_t (y|x) + \nabla_x \ln p_t (x)\right)\right) dt + g(x, t) dw
]
We can train a neural network [math formula: c(x,t)] to learn [math formula: \ln p_t (y|x)]
We can also use some prior knowledge to directly determine [math formula: \nabla_x \ln p_t (y|x)]
## 5. Special Case
If [math formula: \exists \tilde{g}(t) \in \mathbb{R}] s.t. [math formula: g(x, t) = \tilde{g}(t) \cdot I], then the reverse process can be written as:
[math formula: 
dx = \left(f(x, t) - \tilde{g}(t)^2 \nabla_x \ln p_t (x)\right) dt + \tilde{g}(t) dw
]
## 6. Example: Continuous DDPM (Unconditional)
[math formula: 
P(x_t|x_{t-1}) = \mathcal{N} (x_t; \sqrt{1 - \beta_t} \cdot x_{t-1}, \beta_t \cdot I)
]
[math formula: 
x_t = \sqrt{1 - \beta_t} \cdot x_{t-1} + \sqrt{\beta_t} \cdot \epsilon, \quad \epsilon \sim \mathcal{N} (0, I)
]
[math formula: 
x_{t + \Delta t} - x_t = \sqrt{1 - \beta_{t+\Delta t}} \cdot x_t + \sqrt{\beta_{t + \Delta t}} \cdot \epsilon - x_t
]
Because [math formula: \sqrt{1-x} = 1 - \frac{x}{2} + o(x)], we have:
[math formula: 
x_{t + \Delta t} - x_t = - \frac{\beta_{t + \Delta t}}{2} \cdot x_t + \sqrt{\beta_{t + \Delta t}} \cdot \epsilon + o(\beta_{t + \Delta t}) \cdot x_t
]
SDE of forward process(noise):
[math formula: 
dx = - \frac{\beta_t}{2} \cdot x dt + \sqrt{\beta_t} dw
]
SDE of reverse process(denoise):
[math formula: 
dx = \left(- \frac{\beta_t}{2} \cdot x - \beta_t \cdot \nabla_x \ln p_t (x)\right) dt + \sqrt{\beta_t} dw
]

79.content:
---
title: Kabsch-algorithm
---
# Kabsch-algorithm
Kabsch-algorithm 是一种用于计算两个几何体(点集)之间的最佳对齐的算法。
## 目标
两个集合体 [math formula: P,Q \in \mathbb{R}^{N \times 3}]，找到一个[math formula: SE(3)]变换使得，[math formula: P]和[math formula: Q]的距离最小。
默认所有向量都为列向量
[math formula: 
\min_{R,t} l(R, t) = \sum_{i=1}^{N} \| p_i - (Rq_i + t) \|^2 = (p_i - (Rq_i+ t) )^T(p_i - (Rq_i+ t) ), t \in \mathbb{R}^3, R \in SO(3)
]
[math formula: 
\begin{align*}
l &= \sum_{i=1}^{N} \|p_i\|^2 + \| (Rq_i + t) \|^2 - 2p_i^T(Rq_i + t) \\
&= \sum_{i=1}^{N} \|p_i\|^2 + \|Rq_i\|^2 + \|t\|^2 + 2t^T(Rq_i) - 2p_i^T(Rq_i + t) \\
& = \sum_{i=1}^{N} \|p_i\|^2 + \|Rq_i\|^2 + \|t\|^2 + 2t^T(Rq_i - 2p_i) - 2p_i^TRq_i\\
&= \sum_{i=1}^{N} \|p_i\|^2 + \sum_{i=1}^{N} \|Rq_i\|^2  + N\|t\|^2 + 2t^T \sum_{i=1}^{N} (Rq_i - 2p_i) - 2\sum_{i=1}^{N} p_i^TRq_i\\
\frac{\partial l}{\partial t} &= 2Nt + 2 \sum_{i=1}^{N} (Rq_i - 2p_i) = 0 \\
&\Rightarrow t = \frac{1}{N} \sum_{i=1}^{N} (p_i - R q_i)\\
& = \frac{1}{N} \sum_{i=1}^{N} p_i - \frac{1}{N} \sum_{i=1}^{N} R q_i\\
& = \frac{1}{N} \sum_{i=1}^{N} p_i - R \frac{1}{N} \sum_{i=1}^{N}  q_i\\
\end{align*}
]
因此原问题可以先将两个点集的质心对齐，然后计算旋转矩阵。
[math formula: A = P - \frac{1}{N} \sum_{i=1}^{N} p_i]
[math formula: B = Q - \frac{1}{N} \sum_{i=1}^{N} q_i]
最小化目标转化为
[math formula: 
\min_{R} l(R) = \sum_{i=1}^{N} \| p_i' - R q_i' \|^2 \Leftrightarrow \min_{R} \|A - (RB^T)^T\|^2_F
]
为了简化记号，
[math formula: 
\begin{align*}
\|A - (RB^T)^T\|^2_F  &= \|A - BR^T\|^2_F\\
& = Tr((A - BR^T)^T(A - BR^T))\\
&= Tr(A^TA - RB^TA - A^TBR^T + RB^TBR^T)\\
& = Tr(A^TA)  + Tr(B^TB) -2Tr(A^TBR^T)
\end{align*}
]
因此原问题等价于最大化
[math formula: 
\max_{R} Tr(A^TBR^T) = \max_{R} Tr(RB^TA)
]
[math formula: C := B^TA = USV^T] (SVD分解)
[math formula: 
Tr(R C) = Tr(R USV^T) = Tr(V^T R U S)
]
令 [math formula: M = V^T R U]，则有：
[math formula: 
\text{Tr}(RC) = \text{Tr}(MS)
]
### 最大化迹的形式
- [math formula: M] 仍然是正交矩阵，因为 [math formula: V^T R U] 保持正交性。
- [math formula: S] 是对角矩阵，奇异值为 [math formula: s_1, s_2, s_3]。
根据迹的性质：
[math formula: 
\text{Tr}(MS) = \sum_{i=1}^3 M_{ii} s_i
]
此时，我们需要最大化 [math formula: M_{ii} s_i] 的和。
### 利用性质取最大值
由于 [math formula: M] 是正交矩阵，满足：
[math formula: 
-1 \le M_{ii} \le 1
]
为了最大化 [math formula:  \text{Tr}(MS) ]，我们需要 [math formula: M_{ii} = 1]。最优选择是使得：
[math formula: 
M = I \quad \Longrightarrow \quad V^T R U = I \quad \Longrightarrow \quad R = V U^T
]
### 修正旋转矩阵的行列式
旋转矩阵必须满足 [math formula: \det(R) = 1]。然而 [math formula: \det(V U^T)] 的行列式可能为 -1。为了修正这一点，引入一个校正矩阵 [math formula: F]：
[math formula: 
F = \operatorname{diag}(1, 1, \det(V U^T))
]
这样可以确保：
[math formula: 
\det(V F U^T) = \det(V) \cdot \det(F) \cdot \det(U^T) = 1
]
最终最优解为：
[math formula: 
R = V F U^T
]

80.content:
---
title: Matrix Norm
---
All matrix is belong to [math formula:  \mathbb{C}^{m \times n} ] if not specified.
## 1. Vector p norm
[math formula: 
\|x\|_p = \left( \sum_{i=1}^n |x_i|^p \right)^{1/p}
]
## 2. Matrix p norm
[math formula: 
\|A\|_p = \max_{\|x\|_p = 1} \|Ax\|_p
]
## 3. Frobenius norm
[math formula: 
\|A\|_F = \sqrt{\sum_{i=1}^n \sum_{j=1}^n |a_{ij}|^2} = \sqrt{tr(A^H A)} = \sqrt{tr(A A^H)}
]
### Frobenius norm and singular value
[math formula: 
\|A\|_F = \sqrt{\sum_{i=1}^r \sigma_i^2}
]
> proof
>
> [math formula: 
> \|A\|_F = \sqrt{tr(A^T A)} = \sqrt{tr(V \Sigma^2 V^T)} = \sqrt{tr(\Sigma^2)} = \sqrt{\sum_{i=1}^r \sigma_i^2}
> ]
>
> Q.E.D.
### Orthogonal matrix do not change the Frobenius norm
[math formula: 
\|A\|_F = \|J A \|_F = \| A K\|_F, \forall J, K \in \mathbb{R}^{m \times m} \text{ is orthogonal matrix}
]
## 4. Spectral norm(Matrix 2 norm)
[math formula: 
\|A\|_2 = \max_{\|x\|_2 = 1} \|Ax\|_2 = \max_{x} \frac{\|Ax\|_F}{\|x\|_F}
]
### Orthogonal matrix do not change the spectral norm
[math formula: 
\|A\|_2 = \|J A \|_2 = \| A K\|_2, \forall J, K \in \mathbb{R}^{m \times m} \text{ is orthogonal matrix}
]
> proof
>
> [math formula: 
> \|A\|_2 = \max_{\|x\|_2 = 1} \|Ax\|_2 = \max_{x} \frac{\|Ax\|_F}{\|x\|_F} = \max_{x} \frac{\|J A x\|_F}{\|x\|_F} = \|J A \|_2
> ]
>
> similar, [math formula: \|A\|_2 = \| A K\|_2]
> Q.E.D.

81.content:
---
title: auto-grad
---
- 1. Base Principle
- 2. Useful Rules
	- 2.1. Differential
	- 2.2. Trace
- 3. Common differential calculations
	- 3.1. MSE
	- 3.2. Eigenvalue and Eigenvector
		- 3.2.1. Eigenvalue
		- 3.2.2. Eigenvector
## 1. Base Principle
[math formula: l = f(A),f:\mathbb{R}^{m \times n} \to \mathbb{R},f \in \mathcal{C}^1]
[math formula: 
\begin{align*}
\frac{\partial f}{\partial A} = \begin{bmatrix}
\frac{\partial f}{\partial A_{1,1}} & \frac{\partial f}{\partial A_{1,2}} & \cdots & \frac{\partial f}{\partial A_{1,n}} \\
\frac{\partial f}{\partial A_{2,1}} & \frac{\partial f}{\partial A_{2,2}} & \cdots & \frac{\partial f}{\partial A_{2,n}} \\
\vdots & \vdots & \ddots & \vdots \\
\frac{\partial f}{\partial A_{m,1}} & \frac{\partial f}{\partial A_{m,2}} & \cdots & \frac{\partial f}{\partial A_{m,n}}
\end{bmatrix}
\end{align*}
]
[math formula: 
\begin{align*}
d l &= tr(d l)\\
&= tr(d f(A))\\
&= tr(\sum_{i,j} \frac{\partial f}{\partial A_{i,j}} d A_{i,j})\\
&= tr((\frac{\partial f}{\partial A})^T d A)
\end{align*}
]
[math formula: \forall d A, dl = tr(B^T d A) \Longrightarrow \frac{\partial f}{\partial A} = B]
So all we need to do is to find a [math formula: B] that satisfies [math formula: \forall d A, dl = tr(B^T d A)].
## 2. Useful Rules
### 2.1. Differential
- Addition rule: [math formula: d(X \pm Y) = dX \pm dY]
- Product rule: [math formula: d(XY) = (dX)Y + XdY]
- Inverse: [math formula: dX^{-1} = -X^{-1}dXX^{-1}]
- Transpose: [math formula: d(X^T) = (dX)^T]
- Trace: [math formula: d\text{tr}(X) = \text{tr}(dX)]
- Determinant: [math formula: d|X| = \text{tr}(X^\# dX)], where [math formula: X^\#] is the adjugate matrix
- Hadamard product: [math formula: d(X \odot Y) = dX \odot Y + X \odot dY]
- Component-wise(element-wise) function: [math formula: d\sigma(X) = \sigma'(X) \odot dX]
### 2.2. Trace
- Scalar trace: [math formula: a = \text{tr}(a)]
- Transpose: [math formula: \text{tr}(A^T) = \text{tr}(A)]
- Linearity: [math formula: \text{tr}(aA + bB) = a\text{tr}(A) + b\text{tr}(B)]
- Cyclic property: [math formula: \text{tr}(AB) = \text{tr}(BA)], where [math formula: A] and [math formula: B^T] are conformable. Both equal to [math formula: \sum_{i,j} A_{ij}B_{ji}]
- Cyclic property with Hadamard product: [math formula: \text{tr}(A^T(B \odot C)) = \text{tr}((A \odot B)^TC)], where [math formula: A, B, C] have the same dimensions. Both equal to [math formula: \sum_{i,j} A_{ij}B_{ij}C_{ij}]
## 3. Common differential calculations
- [math formula: n] : dimension of output
- [math formula: \bold{\hat{y}}] : predicted value
- [math formula: \bold{y}] : target value
### 3.1. MSE
[math formula: \bold{\hat{y}} = W\bold{x} + \bold{b}]
[math formula: l = \sum_{i=1}^n (y_i - \hat{y}_i)^2 = (\bold{\hat{y}} - \bold{y})^T(\bold{\hat{y}}- \bold{y})]
let [math formula: \bold{t} = \bold{\hat{y}} - \bold{y}],
[math formula: 
dl = tr(dl) = tr(\bold{t}^T d\bold{t} + d\bold{t}^T \bold{t}) =  tr(\bold{t}^T d\bold{t}) + tr(d\bold{t}^T \bold{t}) = tr((2\bold{t})^T d\bold{t})
]
Thus.
[math formula: \frac{\partial l}{\partial \bold{t}} = 2\bold{t}]
[math formula: d \bold{t} = d(\bold{\hat{y}} - \bold{y}) = d\bold{\hat{y}}]
[math formula: \frac{\partial l}{\partial \bold{\hat{y}}} = 2\bold{t}]
[math formula: d \bold{\hat{y}} = d(W\bold{x} + \bold{b}) = dW\bold{x}]
[math formula: dl = tr((\frac{\partial l}{\bold{\partial \hat{y}}})^T dW \bold{x}) = tr(\bold{x} (\frac{\partial l}{\bold{\partial \hat{y}}})^T dW) = tr(((\frac{\partial l}{\bold{\partial \hat{y}}}) \bold{x}^T)^T dW)]
[math formula: \frac{\partial l}{\partial W} = (\frac{\partial l}{\bold{\partial \hat{y}}}) \bold{x}^T] = [math formula: 2\bold{t} \bold{x}^T]
### 3.2. Eigenvalue and Eigenvector
Suppose [math formula: A \in \text{Sym}_n(\mathbb{R})], then [math formula: A] can be decomposed as
[math formula: 
A = Q \Lambda Q^T
]
[math formula: 
\Lambda = \text{diag}(\lambda_1, \lambda_2, \cdots, \lambda_n), \lambda_i \leq \lambda_{i+1}\\
Q=(\mathbf{q}_1, \mathbf{q}_2, \cdots, \mathbf{q}_n) \in \mathbf{O}_n(\mathbb{R}), \text{where } \mathbf{O}_n(\mathbb{R}) = \{\mathbf{M} \in \mathbb{R}^{n \times n} \mid \mathbf{M}^T\mathbf{M} = \mathbf{I}\}
]
where [math formula: \lambda_i] are the eigenvalues of [math formula: A] and [math formula: Q] is the eigenvector matrix.
#### 3.2.1. Eigenvalue
[math formula: 
\begin{align*}
\Lambda &= Q^T A Q\\
d\Lambda &= Q^T dA Q\\
dl &= tr(dl)\\
& =tr((\frac{\partial l}{\partial \Lambda})^T d\Lambda) \\
&= tr((\frac{\partial l}{\partial \Lambda})^T Q^T dA Q)\\
&= tr(Q (\frac{\partial l}{\partial \Lambda})^T Q^T dA)\\
&= tr((Q \frac{\partial l}{\partial \Lambda} Q^T)^T d A)
\end{align*}
]
Thus,
[math formula: 
\frac{\partial l}{\partial A} = Q \frac{\partial l}{\partial \Lambda} Q^T
]
#### 3.2.2. Eigenvector
[math formula: Q^T dQ + dQ^T Q = 0], because [math formula: Q^T Q = \mathbf{I}]
Let [math formula: H = Q^T dQ]
[math formula: 
\begin{align*}
dA &=  dQ \Lambda Q^T + Q d\Lambda Q^T + Q \Lambda dQ^T\\
Q^T dA Q &= (Q^T dQ) \Lambda + d\Lambda +  \Lambda (dQ^TQ)\\
&= H \Lambda + d\Lambda +  \Lambda H\\
\end{align*}
]
[math formula: 
\begin{align*}
(Q^T dA Q)_{ii} &= d \Lambda_{ii}\\
\forall i\not= j, (Q^T dA Q)_{ij} &= H_{ij}(\lambda_j - \lambda_i)\\
\forall i\not= j, H_{ij} &= \frac{1}{\lambda_j - \lambda_i} (Q^T dA Q)_{ij}\\
\forall i, H_{ii} &= 0\\
\end{align*}
]
Let [math formula: F_{ij} = \begin{cases}
	\frac{1}{\lambda_j - \lambda_i} & \text{if } i\not= j\\
	0 & \text{if } i=j
\end{cases}]
[math formula: 
H = F \odot (Q^T dA Q)
]
[math formula: 
\begin{align*}
tr(dl) &= tr(\frac{\partial l}{\partial Q}^T dQ)\\
&= tr(\frac{\partial l}{\partial Q}^T QH)\\
& = tr\left((Q^T \frac{\partial l}{\partial Q})^T (F \odot (Q^T dA Q))\right)\\
& = tr\left( \left((Q^T \frac{\partial l}{\partial Q}) \odot F\right)^T (Q^T dA Q) \right)\\
& = tr\left(Q\ \left( (Q^T\frac{\partial l}{\partial Q}) \odot F \right)^T Q^T d A\right)
\end{align*}
]

82.content:
---
title: svd
---
All matrix is belong to [math formula:  \mathbb{R}^{m \times n} ] if not specified.
## 1. Preliminaries
[math formula: B = A^TA \in \mathbb{R}^{n \times n}] is a symmetric matrix, so it is diagonalizable, and the eigenvalues are non-negative.
[math formula: C = AA^T \in \mathbb{R}^{m \times m}] is also a symmetric matrix, so it is diagonalizable, and the eigenvalues are non-negative.
[math formula: B = V \Lambda_B V^T, V \in \mathbb{R}^{n \times n}]
[math formula: C = U \Lambda_C U^T, U \in \mathbb{R}^{m \times m}]
#### 1.1. proposition
[math formula: B] and [math formula: C] have the same non-zero eigenvalues with the same multiplicity(geometric multiplicity).
> proof
>
> [math formula: 
> B v = A^T A v= \lambda v \Longrightarrow\\
> C Av = A A^T A v = \lambda A v
> ]
>
> if [math formula: \lambda] is an eigenvalue of [math formula: B], then it is also an eigenvalue of [math formula: C].
> similarly,
>
> [math formula: 
> C v = A A^T v = \lambda v \Longrightarrow\\
> B A^T v = A^T A A^T v = \lambda A^T v
> ]
>
> if [math formula: \lambda] is an eigenvalue of [math formula: C], then it is also an eigenvalue of [math formula: B].
> so [math formula: B] and [math formula: C] have the same eigenvalues.
> we analyze the geometric multiplicity of the non-zero eigenvalues.
>
> [math formula: 
> v \in Ker(A^T A - \lambda I) \Longleftrightarrow B v = A^T A v = \lambda v \Longrightarrow
> ]
>
> [math formula: 
> C Av = A A^T A v = \lambda A v \Longleftrightarrow Av \in Ker(A A^T - \lambda I)
> ]
>
> consider the linear map:
>
> [math formula: 
> f: Ker(A^T A - \lambda I) \to Ker(A A^T - \lambda I)\\
> f(v) = Av
> ]
>
> if [math formula: f(v)=Av=\lambda v=0], because [math formula: \lambda \neq 0], so [math formula: v=0].
> so [math formula: f] is injective.
> for any [math formula: w \in Ker(A A^T - \lambda I)], there exists [math formula: v=(1/\lambda)A^T w \in Ker(A^T A - \lambda I)] such that [math formula: f(v)=A (1/\lambda)A^T w = (1/\lambda)A A^T w = (1/\lambda) \lambda w = w].
> so [math formula: f] is surjective.
> so [math formula: f] is an isomorphism.
> Therefore, [math formula: B] and [math formula: C] have the same non-zero eigenvalues with the same geometric multiplicity.
#### 1.2. proposition
[math formula: 
Ker(A) = Ker(A^T A)
]
> proof
>
> [math formula: 
> x \in Ker(A) \Longleftrightarrow A x = 0 \implies A^T A x = 0 \Longleftrightarrow x \in Ker(A^T A)
> ]
>
> [math formula: 
> x \in Ker(A^T A) \Longleftrightarrow A^T A x = 0 \implies x^T A^T A x = 0 \implies A x = 0 \Longleftrightarrow x \in Ker(A)
> ]
>
> Therefore, [math formula: Ker(A) = Ker(A^T A)].
## 2. SVD
[math formula: 
A = U \Sigma V^T
]
[math formula: V = (v_1, v_2, \cdots, v_n)]
[math formula: U = (u_1, u_2, \cdots, u_m)]
[math formula: U = (u_1, u_2, \cdots, u_m) := ((1/\sqrt{\lambda_1}) A v_1, (1/\sqrt{\lambda_2}) A v_2, \cdots, (1/\sqrt{\lambda_r}) A v_r, u_{r+1}, \cdots, u_m)]
[math formula: \Sigma 
= \begin{bmatrix}
diag(\sqrt{\lambda_1}, \sqrt{\lambda_2}, \cdots, \sqrt{\lambda_r}),&0\\
0,&0
\end{bmatrix}]
[math formula: \lambda_i] is the eigenvalue of [math formula: B] and [math formula: C]. [math formula: \lambda_i \geq \lambda_{i+1}]
[math formula: v_i] is the eigenvector of [math formula: B=A^TA], and [math formula: (v_i)_{i=r+1}^{n}] belongs to [math formula: Ker(A^T A)]
[math formula: u_i] is the eigenvector of [math formula: C=AA^T]
[math formula: 
\begin{aligned}
U \Sigma V^T &= \sum_{i=1}^r \sqrt{\lambda_i} u_i v_i^T\\
&= \sum_{i=1}^r \sqrt{\lambda_i} ((1/\sqrt{\lambda_i}) A v_i) v_i^T\\
&= \sum_{i=1}^r A v_i v_i^T\\
&= A \sum_{i=1}^r v_i v_i^T\\
\end{aligned}
]
It is need to prove that [math formula: A \sum_{i=1}^r v_i v_i^T = A]
[math formula: 
\forall x=(x_1, x_2, \cdots, x_n)^T \in \mathbb{R}^n, x = \sum_{i=1}^{n} \alpha_i v_i
]
[math formula: v_i^T x = v_i^T \sum_{i=1}^{n} \alpha_i v_i = \alpha_i]
[math formula: 
x = \sum_{i=1}^{n} \alpha_i v_i = \sum_{i=1}^{n} (v_i^T x) v_i
]
[math formula: 
\begin{aligned}
\forall x, A \sum_{i=1}^r v_i v_i^T x = A \sum_{i=1}^r v_i v_i^T x + A \sum_{i=r+1}^{n} v_i v_i^T x = A x \implies A \sum_{i=1}^r v_i v_i^T = A\\
\end{aligned}
]
Therefore, [math formula: A = U \Sigma V^T = \sum_{i=1}^r \sqrt{\lambda_i} u_i v_i^T]
let [math formula: \sigma_i = \sqrt{\lambda_i}], then [math formula: A = \sum_{i=1}^r \sigma_i u_i v_i^T]

83.content:
---
title: padding_side
---
# 语言模型的padding问题。
## 生成式语言模型（例如decoder-only的模型）
### 训练的时候，一般右侧填充
### 生成的时候向左填充
这个在huggingface的文档里有一个理由:
LLMs are decoder-only architectures, meaning they continue to iterate on your input prompt. If your inputs do not have the same length, they need to be padded. Since LLMs are not trained to continue from pad tokens, your input needs to be left-padded. Make sure you also don’t forget to pass the attention mask to generate!
（原文地址https://huggingface.co/docs/transformers/llm_tutorial#wrong-padding-side ）
经与一位朋友讨论，生成的时候batch_size设置成1可以规避pad side问题，这样可能会使得处理大量数据时生成速度稍慢。
## encoder-（decoder）类型的语言模型（例如机器翻译，文本情感分类）右侧填充
1. 填充右侧的方式为人类一般的隐式习惯
2. 对于文本分类，需要获得每个句子的首个token(cls),如果采取左侧填充，不便于快速获取cls（special classification embedding）的位置
3. 这些任务不需要把输入和输出拼起来。
（参考的https://blog.csdn.net/qq_40965091/article/details/132007023 ）

84.content:
---
title: Coherence
---
Definition, Property and Theorem about Coherence in 《ON THE GENERALIZATION MYSTERY IN DEEP LEARNING》
- 1. Relative work
  - 1.1. Neural networks can memorize random dataset
  - 1.2. Implicit bias
  - 1.3. Uniform convergence may be unable to explain generalization in deep learning.
  - 1.4. What makes a pattern “simple” and why are such patterns reliably learned early
  - 1.5. Comments
- 2. Future work
- 3. Regularization techniques
- 4. Coherence
  - 4.1. Boundedness and Scale Invariance
  - 4.2. Stylized mini-batching
  - 4.3. Effect of zero vectors
  - 4.4. Combining orthogonal distributions
  - 4.5. Bound on Expected Difference
  - 4.6. Decomposition
- 5. Shortcomings
- 6. Generalization Theorem of Coherence
  - 6.1. Gap of Generalization
  - 6.2. Stability
  - 6.3. Stability equals generalization
  - 6.4. Smoothness Assumptions.
  - 6.5. Stability of (Stochastic) Gradient Descent
  - 6.6. 1-Step Expansion
  - 6.7. Unrolling recursion).
  - 6.8. Bound the difference between examples by the coherence as measured by α
  - 6.9. Stability Theorem
  - 6.10. Generalization Theorem
  - 6.11. Corollary: fixed step sizes(learning rate)
  - 6.12. Corollary: step sizes decay linearly
## 1. Relative work
### 1.1. Neural networks can memorize random dataset
Understanding deep learning requires rethinking generalization.
Typical neural networks trained with stochastic gradient descent, which is the usual training method, can easily memorize a random dataset of the same size as the (real) dataset that they were designed for.
### 1.2. Implicit bias
Implicit bias:from among all the models that fit the training set perfectly in an over-parameterized setting, how does gradient descent find one that generalizes well to unseen data when such a model exists
### 1.3. Uniform convergence may be unable to explain generalization in deep learning.
Uniform convergence may be unable to explain generalization in deep learning.
### 1.4. What makes a pattern “simple” and why are such patterns reliably learned early
A Closer Look at Memorization in Deep Networks
In a study on memorization in shallow fully-connected networks and small convolutional networks on mnist and cifar-10, Arpit et al. 2017 discovered that for real datasets, starting from different random initializations, many examples are consistently classified correctly or incorrectly after one epoch of training. They call these easy and hard examples respectively. They hypothesize that this variability of difficulty in real data “is because the easier examples are explained by some simple patterns, which are reliably learned within the first epoch of training.”
### 1.5. Comments
First, the difficulty of an example is not simply a property of that example (whether it has simple patterns or not), but depends on the relationship of that example to others in the training set (what it shares with other examples).
Second, the dynamics of training, including initialization, can determine the difficulty of examples. Consequently, it can accommodate the observed phenomenon of adversarial initialization where examples that are easy to learn with random initialization become significantly harder to learn with a different, specially constructed initialization, and the generalization performance of the network suffers. Any notion of simplicity of patterns intrinsic to an example alone cannot explain adversarial initialization since the dataset remains the same, and therefore, so do the patterns in the data.
Examples learned late by gradient descent (that is, the hard examples), by themselves, are insufficient to define the decision boundary to the same extent that early (or easy) examples are.
## 2. Future work
1. Better metrics for coherence and a tighter bounds.
2. Providing a continuous explanation for the phenomenon of deep double descent
3. nonvacuous bound on the generalization gap
4. Generalization and width.
5. Practical use for new algorithms
## 3. Regularization techniques
- Gradients mean estimation techniques
- View L2 regularization
- Early stopping
## 4. Coherence
[math formula: 
\alpha \equiv \frac{\ell(w+h)-\ell(w)}{\underset{z \sim \mathcal{D}}{\mathbb{E}}\left[\ell_z\left(w+h_z\right)-\ell_z(w)\right]}=\frac{\underset{z \sim \mathcal{D}, z^{\prime} \sim \mathcal{D}}{\mathbb{E}}\left[g_z \cdot g_{z^{\prime}}\right]}{\underset{z \sim \mathcal{D}}{\mathbb{E}}\left[g_z \cdot g_z\right]}=\frac{\underset{z \sim \mathcal{D}}{\mathbb{E}}\left[\left.g_z\right]\right] \underset{z \sim \mathcal{D}}{\mathbb{E}}\left[g_z\right]}{\underset{z \sim \mathcal{D}}{\mathbb{E}}\left[g_z \cdot g_z\right]}
]
[math formula: 
\alpha(\mathcal{V}) \equiv \frac{\underset{v \sim \mathcal{V}, v^{\prime} \sim \mathcal{V}}{\mathbb{E}}\left[v \cdot v^{\prime}\right]}{\underset{v \sim \mathcal{V}}{\mathbb{E}}[v \cdot v]}
]
### 4.1. Boundedness and Scale Invariance
[math formula: 
0 \leq \alpha(\mathcal{V}) \leq 1
]
where [math formula: \alpha(\mathcal{V})=0] iff [math formula: \mathbb{E}_{v \sim \mathcal{V}}[v]=0] and [math formula: \alpha(\mathcal{V})=1] iff all the vectors are equal.
Furthermore, for non-zero [math formula: k \in \mathbb{R}], we have,
[math formula: 
\alpha(k \mathcal{V})=\alpha(\mathcal{V})
]
where [math formula: k \mathcal{V}] denotes the distribution of the random variable [math formula: k v] where [math formula: v] is drawn from [math formula: \mathcal{V}].
### 4.2. Stylized mini-batching
Let [math formula: v_1, v_2, . ., v_k] be [math formula: k] i.i.d. variables drawn from [math formula: \mathcal{V}]. Let [math formula: \mathcal{W}] denote the distribution of the random variable [math formula: w=\frac{1}{k} \sum_{i=1}^k v_i]. We have,
[math formula: 
\alpha(\mathcal{W})=\alpha(k \mathcal{W})=\frac{k \cdot \alpha(\mathcal{V})}{1+(k-1) \cdot \alpha(\mathcal{V})}
]
Furthermore, [math formula: \alpha(\mathcal{W}) \geq \alpha(\mathcal{V})] with equality iff [math formula: \alpha(\mathcal{V})=0] or [math formula: \alpha(\mathcal{V})=1]
When [math formula: \alpha \ll 1 / k] but non-zero (i.e., we have high gradient diversity), creating mini-batches of size [math formula: k] increases coherence almost [math formula: k] times. But, when [math formula: \alpha \approx 1] (i.e., low diversity) there is not much point in creating mini-batches since there is little room for improvement.
### 4.3. Effect of zero vectors
If [math formula: \mathcal{W}] denotes the distribution where with probability [math formula: p>0] we pick a vector from [math formula: \mathcal{V}] and with probability [math formula: 1-p] we pick the zero vector then [math formula: \alpha(\mathcal{W})=p \cdot \alpha(\mathcal{V})]
### 4.4. Combining orthogonal distributions
If [math formula: \mathcal{W}] denotes the distribution where with probability [math formula: p>0] we pick a vector from [math formula: \mathcal{U}] and with probability [math formula: 1-p] we pick a vector from [math formula: \mathcal{V}] and all elements in the support of [math formula: \mathcal{U}] are orthogonal to those in the support of [math formula: \mathcal{V}] then we have
[math formula: 
\alpha(\mathcal{W}) \leq p \cdot \alpha(\mathcal{U})+(1-p) \cdot \alpha(\mathcal{V})
]
### 4.5. Bound on Expected Difference
[math formula: 
\underset{v \sim \mathcal{V}, v^{\prime} \sim \mathcal{V}}{\mathbb{E}}\left[\left\|v-v^{\prime}\right\|\right] \leq \sqrt{2(1-\alpha(\mathcal{V})) \underset{v \sim \mathcal{V}}{\mathbb{E}}[v \cdot v]}
]
### 4.6. Decomposition
Let [math formula: V] be the support of the distribution [math formula: \mathcal{V}]. Furthermore, let
[math formula: 
V=V_1 \oplus V_2 \oplus \cdots \oplus V_k
]
where the subspaces [math formula: V_i(1 \leq i \leq k)] are orthogonal to each other, that is, [math formula: V] is the orthogonal direct sum of the [math formula: V_i . \mathcal{V}] induces a distribution [math formula: \mathcal{V}_i] on each [math formula: V_i]. Suppose each [math formula: \alpha\left(\mathcal{V}_i\right)] exists. Then,
[math formula: 
\alpha(\mathcal{V})=f_1 \cdot \alpha\left(\mathcal{V}_1\right)+f_2 \cdot \alpha\left(\mathcal{V}_2\right)+\cdots+f_k \cdot \alpha\left(\mathcal{V}_k\right)
]
where [math formula: f_i \equiv \mathbb{E}_{v_i \sim \mathcal{V}_i}\left[v_i \cdot v_i\right] /\left(\sum_{i=1}^k \underset{v_i \sim \mathcal{V}_i}{\mathbb{E}}\left[v_i \cdot v_i\right]\right)] and [math formula: 0 \leq f_i \leq 1] and [math formula: \sum_{i=0}^k f_i=1].
## 5. Shortcomings
As a measure of coherence, αm/α⊥m over the whole network, being an average, is a blunt instrument, and therefore, a finer-grained analysis, for example, on a per-layer basis, is sometimes necessary.
## 6. Generalization Theorem of Coherence
### 6.1. Gap of Generalization
[math formula: 
\text{gap}(\mathcal{D}, m) \equiv \mathbb{E}_{S \sim \mathcal{D}^m} \mathbb{E}_\theta \left[ R(A_\theta(S)) - \hat{R}(A_\theta(S), S) \right]
]
### 6.2. Stability
[math formula: 
\text{stab}(\mathcal{D}, m) \equiv \mathbb{E}_{S \sim \mathcal{D}^m} \mathbb{E}_{S' \sim \mathcal{D}^m} \mathbb{E}_\theta \left[ \frac{1}{m} \sum_{i \in [m]} \left[ \ell(A_\theta(S^{(i)}), z_i) - \ell(A_\theta(S), z_i) \right] \right]
]
### 6.3. Stability equals generalization
[math formula: 
\text{gap}(\mathcal{D}, m) = \text{stab}(\mathcal{D}, m).
]
### 6.4. Smoothness Assumptions.
1. We assume that [math formula: \ell(\cdot, z)] is [math formula: L]-Lipschitz and differentiable for every [math formula: z \in Z], that is,
   [math formula: 
   \left|\ell(w, z)-\ell\left(w^{\prime}, z\right)\right| \leq L\left\|w-w^{\prime}\right\|
   ]
   and
   [math formula: 
   \|g(w, z)\| \leq L
   ]
2. We also assume that [math formula: \ell(\cdot, z)] is [math formula: \beta]-smooth for every [math formula: z \in Z]. That is, we assume,
   [math formula: 
   \left\|g(w, z)-g\left(w^{\prime}, z\right)\right\| \leq \beta\left\|w-w^{\prime}\right\|
   ]
### 6.5. Stability of (Stochastic) Gradient Descent
Let [math formula: I_t(θ)] be an indicator variable that is 1 if the ith training example is selected in the mini-batch used at time step t ∈ [T ], and 0 otherwise. Therefore,
[math formula: 
\underset{\theta}{\mathbb{E}}\left[I_t(\theta)\right]=b / m
]
Let [math formula: \delta_t=\left\|w_t-w_t^{\prime}\right\|], and let [math formula: \Delta_t^{\prime \prime}(b)=\frac{1}{b} \sum_{j \in[b]}\left[g\left(w_{t-1}, z_{t j}\right)-g\left(w_{t-1}^{\prime}, z_{t j}^{\prime}\right)\right]]. then
[math formula: 
\delta_t \leq \delta_{t-1}+\eta_t\left\|\Delta_t^{\prime \prime}(b)\right\|
]
### 6.6. 1-Step Expansion
For [math formula: t \in[T]], we have,
[math formula: 
\delta_t \leq\left(1+\eta_t \beta\right) \cdot \delta_{t-1}+\frac{\eta_t I_t(\theta)}{b} \cdot\left\|g\left(w_{t-1}, z_i\right)-g\left(w_{t-1}, z_i^{\prime}\right)\right\|
]
### 6.7. Unrolling recursion).
[math formula: 
\delta_T \leq \sum_{t \in[T]}\left(\frac{\eta_t I_t(\theta)}{b} \cdot\left\|g\left(w_{t-1}, z_i\right)-g\left(w_{t-1}, z_i^{\prime}\right)\right\| \cdot \prod_{k=t+1}^T\left(1+\eta_k \beta\right)\right)
]
### 6.8. Bound the difference between examples by the coherence as measured by α
[math formula: 
\underset{S \sim D^m}{\mathbb{E}} \underset{z_i^{\prime} \sim \mathcal{D}}{\mathbb{E}} \underset{\theta}{\mathbb{E}}\left[\left|\ell\left(\mathbf{A}_\theta\left(S^{(i)}\right), z_i\right)-\ell\left(\mathbf{A}_\theta(S), z_i\right)\right|\right] \leq \frac{L^2}{m} \sum_{t \in[T]}\left[\eta_k \beta\right]_{k=t+1}^T \cdot \eta_t \cdot \sqrt{2\left(1-\alpha\left(w_{t-1}\right)\right)}
]
### 6.9. Stability Theorem
[math formula: 
|\operatorname{stab}(\mathcal{D}, m)| \leq \frac{L^2}{m} \sum_{t \in[T]}\left[\eta_k \beta\right]_{k=t+1}^T \cdot \eta_t \cdot \sqrt{2\left(1-\alpha\left(w_{t-1}\right)\right)}
]
### 6.10. Generalization Theorem
[math formula: 
|\operatorname{gap}(\mathcal{D}, m)| \leq \frac{L^2}{m} \sum_{t \in[T]}\left[\eta_k \beta\right]_{k=t+1}^T \cdot \eta_t \cdot \sqrt{2\left(1-\alpha\left(w_{t-1}\right)\right)}
]
### 6.11. Corollary: fixed step sizes(learning rate)
If the step sizes are fixed, that is, [math formula: \eta_t=\eta] then
[math formula: 
|\operatorname{gap}(\mathcal{D}, m)| \leq \frac{L^2 \eta}{m} \sum_{t \in[T]} \exp ((T-t) \eta \beta) \cdot \sqrt{2\left(1-\alpha\left(w_{t-1}\right)\right)}
]
### 6.12. Corollary: step sizes decay linearly
If we assume as in Hardt et al. [2016] that step sizes decay linearly, that is, for some [math formula: \eta>0] we have [math formula: \eta_t \leq \eta / t] then
[math formula: 
|\operatorname{gap}(\mathcal{D}, m)| \leq \frac{L^2 \eta T^{\eta \beta}}{m} \sum_{t \in[T]} \sqrt{2\left(1-\alpha\left(w_{t-1}\right)\right)}
]

86.content:
---
title: posterior mean
---
### Tweedie's formula
---
Let [math formula: p(\mathbf{y}|\mathbf{\eta})] belong to the exponential family distribution
[math formula: p(\mathbf{y}|\mathbf{\eta}) = p_0(\mathbf{y}) \exp(\mathbf{\eta}^\top T(\mathbf{y}) - \varphi(\mathbf{\eta}))]
where [math formula: \mathbf{\eta}] is the canonical vector of the family, [math formula: T(\mathbf{y})] is some function of [math formula: \mathbf{y}], and [math formula: \varphi(\mathbf{\eta})] is the cumulant generation function which normalizes the density, and [math formula: p_0(\mathbf{y})] is the density up to the scale factor when [math formula: \mathbf{\eta} = 0]. Then, the posterior mean [math formula: \hat{\mathbf{\eta}} := \mathbb{E}[\mathbf{\eta}|\mathbf{y}]] should satisfy
[math formula: (\nabla_{\mathbf{y}} T(\mathbf{y}))^\top \hat{\mathbf{\eta}} = \nabla_{\mathbf{y}} \log p(\mathbf{y}) - \nabla_{\mathbf{y}} \log p_0(\mathbf{y})]
### Posterior mean of VP-SDE (continuous DDPM)
---
For the case of VP-SDE or DDPM sampling, [math formula: p(\mathbf{x}_0|\mathbf{x}_t)] has the unique posterior mean at
[math formula: 
\hat{\mathbf{x}}_0 := \mathbb{E}[\mathbf{x}_0|\mathbf{x}_t] = \frac{1}{\sqrt{\bar{\alpha}(t)}}(\mathbf{x}_t + (1 - \bar{\alpha}(t))\nabla_{\mathbf{x}_t}\log p_t(\mathbf{x}_t))
]
**Proof.** For the case of VP-SDE or DDPM forward sampling, we have
[math formula: 
p(\mathbf{x}_t|\mathbf{x}_0) = \mathcal{N}(\mathbf{x}_t; \sqrt{\bar{\alpha}(t)}\mathbf{x}_0, (1 - \bar{\alpha}(t))I)
]
[math formula: 
p(\mathbf{x}_t|\mathbf{x}_0) = \frac{1}{(2\pi(1 - \bar{\alpha}(t)))^{d/2}}\exp\left(-\frac{\|\mathbf{x}_t - \sqrt{\bar{\alpha}(t)}\mathbf{x}_0\|^2}{2(1 - \bar{\alpha}(t))}\right),
]
which is a Gaussian distribution. The corresponding canonical decomposition is then given by
[math formula: 
p(\mathbf{x}_t|\mathbf{x}_0) = p_0(\mathbf{x}_t)\exp\left(\mathbf{x}_0^\top T(\mathbf{x}_t) - \varphi(\mathbf{x}_0)\right),
]
where
[math formula: 
\begin{align*}
p_0(\mathbf{x}_t) &:= \frac{1}{(2\pi(1 - \bar{\alpha}(t)))^{d/2}}\exp\left(-\frac{\|\mathbf{x}_t\|^2}{2(1 - \bar{\alpha}(t))}\right) \\
T(\mathbf{x}_t) &:= \frac{\sqrt{\bar{\alpha}(t)}}{1 - \bar{\alpha}(t)}\mathbf{x}_t \\
\varphi(\mathbf{x}_0) &:= \frac{\bar{\alpha}(t)\|\mathbf{x}_0\|^2}{2(1 - \bar{\alpha}(t))}
\end{align*}
]
Therefore, we have
[math formula: 
\frac{\sqrt{\bar{\alpha}(t)}}{1 - \bar{\alpha}(t)}\hat{\mathbf{x}}_0 = \nabla_{\mathbf{x}_t}\log p_t(\mathbf{x}_t) + \frac{1}{1 - \bar{\alpha}(t)}\mathbf{x}_t
]
which leads to
[math formula: 
\hat{\mathbf{x}}_0 = \frac{1}{\sqrt{\bar{\alpha}(t)}}(\mathbf{x}_t + (1 - \bar{\alpha}(t))\nabla_{\mathbf{x}_t}\log p_t(\mathbf{x}_t))
]
This concludes the proof.
### Posterior mean of General SDE
A general SDE of diffusion is given by
[math formula: 
d \mathbf{x} = \mathbf{f}(\mathbf{x}, t)dt + G(t) d\mathbf{w}_t
]
where [math formula: \mathbf{f}: \mathbb{R}^d \times [0, T] \to \mathbb{R}^d] and [math formula: G: [0, T] \to \mathbb{R}^{d \times d}] are given functions.

---
title: transformer
---
- transformer 结构
- transformer 的掩码
- transformer 中 dropout 出现的地方
- transformer 中 layer\_norm 出现的地方
- torch.nn.Transformer 的 mask 使用
- 句子的处理流程
# transformer 结构
# transformer 的掩码
| 注意力方式 | 掩码                                   |
| ---------- | -------------------------------------- |
| 自注意力   | src_mask = src_att_mask + src_pad_mask |
| 自注意力   | tgt_mask = tgt_att_mask + tgt_pad_mask |
| 交叉注意力 | mem_mask = mem_att_mask + mem_pad_mask |
| 掩码类型     | 作用                                                                         |
| ------------ | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| \*\_pad_mask | 为了让当前单词不注意到填充的单词.                                            |
| \*\_att_mask | 为了让当前单词不注意到它“不该”注意到的单词。例如 tgt_att_mask 为上三角矩阵。 |
| 掩码类型 | 初始形状              | 拓展后的形状                         |
| -------- | --------------------- | ------------------------------------ |
| src_mask | (batch_size, src_len) | (batch_size, head, src_len, src_len) |
| tgt_mask | (batch_size, tgt_len) | (batch_size, head, tgt_len, tgt_len) |
| mem_mask | (batch_size, src_len) | (batch_size, head, tgt_len, src_len) |
# transformer 中 dropout 出现的地方
1. 位置嵌入之后。
2. 注意力 softmax 之后。
3. 多头注意力之后的线性变换后。
4. feed_forward 第二个线性层之后。
# transformer 中 layer_norm 出现的地方
1. 残差块之前（或之后）
2. （最后一个 Decoder 之后）
# torch.nn.Transformer 的 mask 使用
Binary and float masks are supported. For a binary mask, a True value indicates that the corresponding position is not allowed to attend. For a float mask, the mask values will be added to the attention weight.
If both attn_mask and key_padding_mask are supplied, their types should match.
# 句子的处理流程

---
title: Gaussian Distribution
---
## One-dimensional Gaussian Distribution
[math formula: 
p(x) = \mathcal{N}(\mu, \sigma^2) = \frac{1}{\sigma\sqrt{2\pi}}\,
\exp\!\left(-\frac{(x-\mu)^{2}}{2\sigma^{2}}\right),x\in \mathbb{R}, \mu\in \mathbb{R}, \sigma>0
]
## Multi-dimensional Gaussian Distribution
A random vector is said to be k-variate normally distributed if every linear combination of its k components has a univariate normal distribution.
## KL Divergence between two Gaussian Distributions
[math formula: 
p_1(\mathbf{x}) = \mathcal{N}(\mathbf{x};\mu_1, \mathbf{\Sigma}_1), p_2(\mathbf{x}) = \mathcal{N}(\mathbf{x};\mu_2, \mathbf{\Sigma}_2), \mathbf{x}\in \mathbb{R}^d
]
[math formula: 
D_{KL}(p_1||p_2) = \frac{1}{2} \left( \log \frac{|\Sigma_2|}{|\Sigma_1|} + \text{Tr}(\Sigma_2^{-1}\Sigma_1) + (\mu_2 - \mu_1)^T\Sigma_2^{-1}(\mu_2 - \mu_1) - d \right)
]

---
title: SE(3)
---
## Invariance and Equivariance
### Invariance
Let [math formula: A=\{f\mid \text{ f is a function from X to Y}\}] be a set of functions.
[math formula: g:X\to Y] is invariant under [math formula: A] if [math formula: g(x)=g(f(x))] for all [math formula: f\in A] and [math formula: x\in X], denoted as [math formula: g\in \text{A-Invariant}].
### Equivariance
Let [math formula: A=\{f\mid \text{ f is a function from X to Y}\}] be a set of functions.
[math formula: g:X\to Y] is equivariant under [math formula: A] if [math formula: g(f(x))=f(g(x))] for all [math formula: f\in A] and [math formula: x\in X], denoted as [math formula: g\in \text{A-Equivariant}].
## Group in [math formula: \mathbb{R}^3]
- [math formula: GL(3) = \{M \in \mathbb{R}^{3 \times 3} \mid M \text{ is invertible}\}]
- [math formula: O(3) = \{M \in \mathbb{R}^{3 \times 3} \mid MM^T = M^T M = I\}]
- [math formula: SO(3) = \{M \in \mathbb{R}^{3 \times 3} \mid MM^T = M^T M = I \wedge \det(M) = 1\}]
> [math formula: SO(3) < O(3) < GL(3)]
- [math formula: SE(3) = \{f \mid f(x) = Rx + t \wedge R \in SO(3) \wedge t \in \mathbb{R}^3\}]
> [math formula: SO(3) < SE(3)]
### SE(3)-Equivariance
- A function [math formula: g] is SE(3)-equivariant if and only if for any [math formula: f \in SE(3)],
  [math formula: g \circ f(x) = f \circ g(x)]
> **Property**: The composition of two SE(3)-equivariant functions is also SE(3)-equivariant.
### Common SO(3)-Invariant functions
#### Norm in [math formula: \mathbb{R}^n]
[math formula: 
f(x) = \|x\| = \langle x, x \rangle = (x^T U^T)(U x) = x^T x
]

90.content:
---
title: martingale
---
## Definition
- Probability space: a triple [math formula: (\Omega, \Sigma, \mathbb{P})], where [math formula: \Omega] is a set, [math formula: \Sigma] is a sigma-algebra on [math formula: \Omega], and [math formula: \mathbb{P}] is a probability measure on [math formula: \Sigma].
- Stochastic process: a collection of random variables indexed by a set, usually denoted as [math formula: \{X_t: t \in T\}] or [math formula: \{X(t): t \in T\}]
  In stochastic process, [math formula: T] often is a total ordered index set (e.g. [math formula: \mathbb{N}, \mathbb{Z}, \mathbb{R}])
- Banach space: a complete normed vector space.
- Filtration: Let [math formula: (\Omega, \Sigma, \mathbb{P})] be a probability space, [math formula: I] be a total ordered index set, [math formula: \mathbb{F}=\{\Sigma_i : i \in I \wedge \Sigma_i \text{ is a sub-sigma-algebra of } \Sigma\}], We say [math formula: \mathbb{F}] is a filtration if [math formula: \Sigma_i \subseteq \Sigma_j] for all [math formula: i < j]. [math formula: (\Omega, \Sigma, \mathbb{P}, \mathbb{F})] is called a filtered probability space.
- Adapted to the filtration: Let [math formula: (\Omega, \Sigma, \mathbb{P})] be a probability space, [math formula: T] be a total ordered index set, [math formula: \mathbb{F}=\{\Sigma_i: i\in T\}] be a filtration of [math formula: \Sigma], a stochastic process [math formula: X: T \times \Omega \rightarrow S] is called adapted to the filtration [math formula: \mathbb{F}] if for each [math formula: t \in T], [math formula: X_t] is a [math formula: \Sigma_t]-measurable function.
### Martingale
In full generality, a stochastic process [math formula: Y: T \times \Omega \rightarrow S] taking values in a Banach space [math formula: S] with norm [math formula: \|\cdot\|_S] is a **martingale** with respect to a filtration [math formula: \Sigma_*] and probability measure [math formula: \mathbb{P}] if
- [math formula: \Sigma_*] is a filtration of the underlying probability space [math formula: (\Omega, \Sigma, \mathbb{P})];
- [math formula: Y] is adapted to the filtration [math formula: \Sigma_*], i.e., for each [math formula: t] in the index set [math formula: T], the random variable [math formula: Y_t] is a [math formula: \Sigma_t]-measurable function;
- for each [math formula: t], [math formula: Y_t] lies in the [math formula: L^p] space [math formula: L^1(\Omega, \Sigma_t, \mathbb{P}; S)], i.e.
  [math formula: 
      \mathbb{E}_{\mathbb{P}}(\|Y_t\|_S) < +\infty;
  ]
- for all [math formula: s] and [math formula: t] with [math formula: s < t] and all [math formula: F \in \Sigma_s],
  [math formula: 
      \mathbb{E}_{\mathbb{P}}\left([Y_t - Y_s] \chi_F\right) = 0,
  ]
  where [math formula: \chi_F] denotes the indicator function of the event [math formula: F].
  In Grimmett and Stirzaker's _Probability and Random Processes_, this last condition is denoted as
  [math formula: 
  Y_s = \mathbb{E}_{\mathbb{P}}(Y_t \mid \Sigma_s),
  ]
  which is a general form of conditional expectation.

91.content:
---
title: 概率论-集中不等式
---
从维基百科和机器学习的书上整理、复制、粘贴了几个常见的集中不等式
- Markov's inequality
  - statement
  - intuition
  - proof
  - corollaries
- Chebyshev's inequality
  - statement
  - proof
- Hoeffding's inequality
  - Hoeffding’s lemma
    - statement
    - Proof
  - proof
- McDiarmid’s inequality
  - statement
  - Definition Martingale Difference
  - Lemma
  - Theorem Azuma's inequality
    - proof
  - proof
# Markov's inequality
## statement
If [math formula: X] is a nonnegative random variable and [math formula: a>0], then the probability that [math formula: X] is at least [math formula: a] is at most the expectation of [math formula: X] divided by [math formula: a]
[math formula: 
\mathrm{P}(X \geq a) \leq \frac{\mathrm{E}(X)}{a}
]
## intuition
[math formula: \mathrm{E}(X)=\mathrm{P}(X<a) \cdot \mathrm{E}(X \mid X < a)+\mathrm{P}(X \geq a) \cdot \mathrm{E}(X \mid X \geq a)]
[math formula: \mathrm{E}(X) \geq \mathrm{P}(X \geq a) \cdot \mathrm{E}(X \mid X \geq a) \geq a \cdot \mathrm{P}(X \geq a)]
[math formula: \mathrm{P}(X \geq a) \leq \frac{\mathrm{E}(X)}{a}]
## proof
For any event [math formula: E], let [math formula: I_E] be the indicator random variable of [math formula: E], that is, [math formula: I_E=1] if [math formula: E] occurs and [math formula: I_E=0] otherwise.
Using this notation, we have [math formula: I_{(X \geq a)}=1] if the event [math formula: X \geq a] occurs, and [math formula: I_{(X \geq a)}=0] if [math formula: X<a]. Then, given [math formula: a>0],
[math formula: 
a I_{(X \geq a)} \leq X
]
which is clear if we consider the two possible values of [math formula: X \geq a]. If [math formula: X<a], then [math formula: I_{(X \geq a)}=0], and so [math formula: a I_{(X \geq a)}=0 \leq X].
Otherwise, we have [math formula: X \geq a], for which [math formula: I_{X \geq a}=1] and so [math formula: a I_{X \geq a}=a \leq X].
Since [math formula: \mathrm{E}] is a monotonically increasing function, taking expectation of both sides of an inequality cannot reverse it. Therefore,
[math formula: 
\mathrm{E}\left(a I_{(X \geq a)}\right) \leq \mathrm{E}(X) .
]
Now, using linearity of expectations, the left side of this inequality is the same as
[math formula: 
a \mathrm{E}\left(I_{(X \geq a)}\right)=a(1 \cdot \mathrm{P}(X \geq a)+0 \cdot \mathrm{P}(X<a))=a \mathrm{P}(X \geq a) .
]
Thus we have
[math formula: 
a \mathrm{P}(X \geq a) \leq \mathrm{E}(X)
]
and since [math formula: a>0], we can divide both sides by [math formula: a].
## corollaries
[math formula: \phi] is a nondecreasing nonnegative fundction
[math formula: 
\mathrm{P}(|X| \geq a)=\mathrm{P}(\varphi(|X|) \geq \varphi(a)) \stackrel{\mathrm{MI}}{\leq} \frac{\mathrm{E}(\varphi(|X|))}{\varphi(a)}
]
# Chebyshev's inequality
## statement
[math formula: 
\mathrm{P}(|X-\mathrm{E}(X)| \geq a) \leq \frac{\operatorname{Var}(X)}{a^2},
]
## proof
for any [math formula: a>0]
[math formula: 
\operatorname{Var}(X)=\mathrm{E}\left[(X-\mathrm{E}(X))^2\right] .
]
because Markov's inequality
[math formula: 
\mathrm{P}\left((X-\mathrm{E}(X))^2 \geq a^2\right) \leq \frac{\operatorname{Var}(X)}{a^2} .
]
This argument can be summarized (where "MI" indicates use of Markov's inequality):
[math formula: 
\mathrm{P}(|X-\mathrm{E}(X)| \geq a)=\mathrm{P}\left((X-\mathrm{E}(X))^2 \geq a^2\right) \stackrel{\mathrm{MI}}{\leq} \frac{\mathrm{E}\left((X-\mathrm{E}(X))^2\right)}{a^2}=\frac{\operatorname{Var}(X)}{a^2} .
]
# Hoeffding's inequality
Let [math formula: X_1, \ldots, X_m] be independent random variables with [math formula: X_i] taking values in [math formula: \left[a_i, b_i\right]] for all [math formula: i \in[1, m]]. Then for any [math formula: \epsilon>0], the following inequalities hold for [math formula: S_m=\sum_{i=1}^m X_i] :
[math formula: 
\begin{gathered}
\operatorname{Pr}\left[S_m-\mathrm{E}\left[S_m\right] \geq \epsilon\right] \leq e^{-2 \epsilon^2 / \sum_{i=1}^m\left(b_i-a_i\right)^2} \\
\operatorname{Pr}\left[S_m-\mathrm{E}\left[S_m\right] \leq-\epsilon\right] \leq e^{-2 \epsilon^2 / \sum_{i=1}^m\left(b_i-a_i\right)^2} .
\end{gathered}
]
## Hoeffding’s lemma
### statement
Let [math formula: X] be a random variable with [math formula: E[X]=0] and [math formula: a \leq X \leq b] with [math formula: b>a]. Then, for any [math formula: t>0], the following inequality holds:
[math formula: 
\mathrm{E}\left[e^{t X}\right] \leq e^{\frac{t^2(b-a)^2}{8}} .
]
### Proof
By the convexity of [math formula: x \mapsto e^x], for all [math formula: x \in[a, b]], the following holds:
[math formula: 
e^{t x} \leq \frac{b-x}{b-a} e^{t a}+\frac{x-a}{b-a} e^{t b} .
]
Thus, using [math formula: \mathrm{E}[X]=0],
[math formula: 
\mathrm{E}\left[e^{t X}\right] \leq \mathrm{E}\left[\frac{b-X}{b-a} e^{t a}+\frac{X-a}{b-a} e^{t b}\right]=\frac{b}{b-a} e^{t a}+\frac{-a}{b-a} e^{t b}=e^{\phi(t)},
]
where,
[math formula: 
\phi(t)=\log \left(\frac{b}{b-a} e^{t a}+\frac{-a}{b-a} e^{t b}\right)=t a+\log \left(\frac{b}{b-a}+\frac{-a}{b-a} e^{t(b-a)}\right) .
]
For any [math formula: t>0], the first and second derivative of [math formula: \phi] are given below:
[math formula: 
\begin{aligned}
\phi^{\prime}(t) & =a-\frac{a e^{t(b-a)}}{\frac{b}{b-a}-\frac{a}{b-a} e^{t(b-a)}}=a-\frac{a}{\frac{b}{b-a} e^{-t(b-a)}-\frac{a}{b-a}}, \\
\phi^{\prime \prime}(t) & =\frac{-a b e^{-t(b-a)}}{\left[\frac{b}{b-a} e^{-t(b-a)}-\frac{a}{b-a}\right]^2} \\
& =\frac{\alpha(1-\alpha) e^{-t(b-a)}(b-a)^2}{\left[(1-\alpha) e^{-t(b-a)}+\alpha\right]^2} \\
& =\frac{\alpha}{\left[(1-\alpha) e^{-t(b-a)}+\alpha\right]} \frac{(1-\alpha) e^{-t(b-a)}}{\left[(1-\alpha) e^{-t(b-a)}+\alpha\right]}(b-a)^2 .
\end{aligned}
]
where [math formula: \alpha] denotes [math formula: \frac{-a}{b-a}]. Note that [math formula: \phi(0)=\phi^{\prime}(0)=0] and that [math formula: \phi^{\prime \prime}(t)=u(1-u)(b-a)^2] where [math formula: u=\frac{\alpha}{\left[(1-\alpha) e^{-t(b-a)}+\alpha\right]}]. Since [math formula: u] is in [math formula: [0,1], u(1-u)] is upper bounded by [math formula: 1 / 4] and [math formula: \phi^{\prime}(t) \leq \frac{(b-a)^2}{4}]. Thus, by the second order expansion of function [math formula: \phi], there exists [math formula: \theta \in[0, t]] such that:
[math formula: 
\phi(t)=\phi(0)+t \phi^{\prime}(0)+\frac{t^2}{2} \phi^{\prime \prime}(\theta) \leq t^2 \frac{(b-a)^2}{8},
]
## proof
[math formula: \begin{aligned} \operatorname{Pr}\left[S_m-\mathrm{E}\left[S_m\right] \geq \epsilon\right] & \leq e^{-t \epsilon} \mathrm{E}\left[e^{t\left(S_m-\mathrm{E}\left[S_m\right]\right)}\right] \\ & =\Pi_{i=1}^m e^{-t \epsilon} \mathrm{E}\left[e^{t\left(X_i-\mathrm{E}\left[X_i\right]\right)}\right] \\ & \leq \Pi_{i=1}^m e^{-t \epsilon} e^{t^2\left(b_i-a_i\right)^2 / 8} \\ & =e^{-t \epsilon} e^{t^2 \sum_{i=1}^m\left(b_i-a_i\right)^2 / 8} \\ & \leq e^{-2 \epsilon^2 / \sum_{i=1}^m\left(b_i-a_i\right)^2},\end{aligned}]
# McDiarmid’s inequality
## statement
Let [math formula: X_1, \ldots, X_m \in \mathcal{X}^m] be a set of [math formula: m \geq 1] independent random variables and assume that there exist [math formula: c_1, \ldots, c_m>0] such that [math formula: f: \mathcal{X}^m \rightarrow \mathbb{R}] satisfies the following conditions:
[math formula: 
\left|f\left(x_1, \ldots, x_i, \ldots, x_m\right)-f\left(x_1, \ldots, x_i^{\prime}, \ldots x_m\right)\right| \leq c_i,
]
for all [math formula: i \in[1, m]] and any points [math formula: x_1, \ldots, x_m, x_i^{\prime} \in \mathcal{X}]. Let [math formula: f(S)] denote [math formula: f\left(X_1, \ldots, X_m\right)], then, for all [math formula: \epsilon>0], the following inequalities hold:
[math formula: 
\begin{gathered}
\operatorname{Pr}[f(S)-\mathrm{E}[f(S)] \geq \epsilon] \leq \exp \left(\frac{-2 \epsilon^2}{\sum_{i=1}^m c_i^2}\right) \\
\operatorname{Pr}[f(S)-\mathrm{E}[f(S)] \leq-\epsilon] \leq \exp \left(\frac{-2 \epsilon^2}{\sum_{i=1}^m c_i^2}\right) .
\end{gathered}
]
## Definition Martingale Difference
A sequence of random variables [math formula: V_1, V_2, \ldots] is a martingale difference sequence with respect to [math formula: X_1, X_2, \ldots] if for all [math formula: i>0, V_i] is a function of [math formula: X_1, \ldots, X_i] and
[math formula: 
\mathrm{E}\left[V_{i+1} \mid X_1, \ldots, X_i\right]=0 .
]
## Lemma
Let [math formula: V] and [math formula: Z] be random variables satisfying [math formula: \mathrm{E}[V \mid Z]=0] and, for some function [math formula: f] and constant [math formula: c \geq 0], the inequalities:
[math formula: 
f(Z) \leq V \leq f(Z)+c .
]
Then, for all [math formula: t>0], the following upper bound holds:
[math formula: 
\mathrm{E}\left[e^{t V} \mid Z\right] \leq e^{t^2 c^2 / 8} .
]
## Theorem Azuma's inequality
Let [math formula: V_1, V_2, \ldots] be a martingale difference sequence with respect to the random variables [math formula: X_1, X_2, \ldots], and assume that for all [math formula: i>0] there is a constant [math formula: c_i \geq 0] and
random variable [math formula: Z_i], which is a function of [math formula: X_1, \ldots, X_{i-1}], that satisfy
[math formula: 
Z_i \leq V_i \leq Z_i+c_i .
]
Then, for all [math formula: \epsilon>0] and [math formula: m], the following inequalities hold:
[math formula: 
\begin{gathered}
\operatorname{Pr}\left[\sum_{i=1}^m V_i \geq \epsilon\right] \leq \exp \left(\frac{-2 \epsilon^2}{\sum_{i=1}^m c_i^2}\right) \\
\operatorname{Pr}\left[\sum_{i=1}^m V_i \leq-\epsilon\right] \leq \exp \left(\frac{-2 \epsilon^2}{\sum_{i=1}^m c_i^2}\right) .
\end{gathered}
]
### proof
Proof For any [math formula: k \in[1, m]], let [math formula: S_k=\sum_{i=1}^k V_k]. Then, using Chernoff's bounding technique, for any [math formula: t>0], we can write
[math formula: 
\begin{aligned}
\operatorname{Pr}\left[S_m \geq \epsilon\right] & \leq e^{-t \epsilon} \mathrm{E}\left[e^{t S_m}\right] \\
& =e^{-t \epsilon} \mathrm{E}\left[e^{t S_{m-1}} \mathrm{E}\left[e^{t \mathbf{V}_m} \mid X_1, \ldots, X_{m-1}\right]\right] \\
& \leq e^{-t \epsilon} \mathrm{E}\left[e^{t S_{m-1}}\right] e^{t^2 c_m^2 / 8} \\
& \leq e^{-t \epsilon} e^{t^2 \sum_{i=1}^m c_i^2 / 8} \\
& =e^{-2 \epsilon^2 / \sum_{i=1}^m c_i^2}
\end{aligned}
]
where we chose [math formula: t=4 \epsilon / \sum_{i=1}^m c_i^2] to minimize the upper bound. This proves the first statement of the theorem, and the second statement is shown in a similar way.
([math formula: if\ F1\subset F2,\ then\ E[E[Y|F1]|F2] = E[E[Y|F2]|F1] = E[Y|F1]])
## proof
Define a sequence of random variables [math formula: V_k, k \in[1, m]], as follows: [math formula: V=f(S)-\mathrm{E}[f(S)], V_1=\mathrm{E}\left[V \mid X_1\right]-\mathrm{E}[V]], and for [math formula: k>1],
[math formula: 
V_k=\mathrm{E}\left[V \mid X_1, \ldots, X_k\right]-\mathrm{E}\left[V \mid X_1, \ldots, X_{k-1}\right] .
]
Note that [math formula: V=\sum_{k=1}^m V_k]. Furthermore, the random variable [math formula: \mathrm{E}\left[V \mid X_1, \ldots, X_k\right]] is a function of [math formula: X_1, \ldots, X_k]. Conditioning on [math formula: X_1, \ldots, X_{k-1}] and taking its expectation is therefore:
[math formula: 
\mathrm{E}\left[\mathrm{E}\left[V \mid X_1, \ldots, X_k\right] \mid X_1, \ldots, X_{k-1}\right]=\mathrm{E}\left[V \mid X_1, \ldots, X_{k-1}\right],
]
which implies [math formula: \mathrm{E}\left[V_k \mid X_1, \ldots, X_{k-1}\right]=0]. Thus, the sequence [math formula: \left(V_k\right)_{k \in[1, m]}] is a martingale difference sequence. Next, observe that, since [math formula: \mathrm{E}[f(S)]] is a scalar, [math formula: V_k] can be expressed as follows:
[math formula: 
V_k=\mathrm{E}\left[f(S) \mid X_1, \ldots, X_k\right]-\mathrm{E}\left[f(S) \mid X_1, \ldots, X_{k-1}\right] .
]
Now define the random variables for each [math formula: i]
[math formula: 
\begin{aligned}
U_i & :=\sup _{x \in \mathcal{X}_i} \mathbb{E}\left[f\left(X_{1 \rightharpoondown(i-1)}, x, X_{(i+1) \rightharpoondown n}\right) \mid X_{1 \rightharpoondown(i-1)}, X_i=x\right]-\left[f\left(X_{1 \rightharpoondown(i-1)}, X_{i \rightharpoondown n}\right) \mid X_{1 \rightharpoondown(i-1)}\right], \\
L_i & :=\inf _{x \in \mathcal{X}_i} \mathbb{E}\left[f\left(X_{1 \rightharpoondown(i-1)}, x, X_{(i+1) \rightharpoondown n}\right) \mid X_{1 \rightharpoondown(i-1)}, X_i=x\right]-\left[f\left(X_{1 \rightharpoondown(i-1)}, X_{i \rightharpoondown n}\right) \mid X_{1 \rightharpoondown(i-1)}\right] .
\end{aligned}
]
Since [math formula: X_i, \ldots, X_n] are independent of each other, conditioning on [math formula: X_i=x] does not affect the probabilities of the other variables, so these are equal to the expressions
[math formula: 
\begin{aligned}
U_i & =\sup _{x \in \mathcal{X}_i} \mathbb{E}\left[f\left(X_{1 \rightarrow(i-1)}, x, X_{(i+1) \rightharpoondown n}\right)-f\left(X_{1 \rightharpoondown(i-1)}, X_{i \rightarrow n}\right) \mid X_{1 \rightarrow(i-1)}\right], \\
L_i & =\inf _{x \in \mathcal{X}_i} \mathbb{E}\left[f\left(X_{1 \rightarrow(i-1)}, x, X_{(i+1) \rightharpoondown n}\right)-f\left(X_{1 \rightarrow(i-1)}, X_{i \rightharpoondown n}\right) \mid X_{1 \rightarrow(i-1)}\right] .
\end{aligned}
]
[math formula: 
\begin{aligned}
U_i-L_i & =\sup _{u \in \mathcal{X}_i, \ell \in \mathcal{X}_i} \mathbb{E}\left[f\left(X_{1 \rightharpoondown(i-1)}, u, X_{(i+1) \rightharpoondown n}\right) \mid X_{1 \rightharpoondown(i-1)}\right]-\mathbb{E}\left[f\left(X_{1 \rightharpoondown(i-1)}, \ell, X_{(i+1) \rightharpoondown n}\right) \mid X_{1 \rightharpoondown(i-1)}\right] \\
& =\sup _{u \in \mathcal{X}_i, \ell \in \mathcal{X}_i} \mathbb{E}\left[f\left(X_{1 \rightarrow(i-1)}, u, X_{(i+1) \rightharpoondown n}\right)-f\left(X_{1 \rightarrow(i-1)}, l, X_{(i+1) \rightharpoondown n}\right) \mid X_{1 \rightharpoondown(i-1)}\right] \\
& \leq \sup _{x_u \in \mathcal{X}_i, x_l \in \mathcal{X}_i} \mathbb{E}\left[c_i \mid X_{1 \rightharpoondown(i-1)}\right] \\
& \leq c_i
\end{aligned}
]
[math formula: L_i] is a random variables about [math formula: X_1,X2,...,X_{i-1}]
thus, [math formula: L_k \leq V_k \leq L_k+c_k]. In view of these inequalities, we can apply Azuma's inequality to [math formula: V=\sum_{k=1}^m V_k]

92.content:
# Kronecker-factored Approximate
- Kronecker-factored Approximate
  - 1. 约定
  - 2. 目标
    - 2.1. 证明
  - 3. Kronecker
    - 3.1. Definition
    - 3.2. Properties
  - 4. Useful formula
## 1. 约定
默认所有向量都是列向量
## 2. 目标
求[math formula: F^{-1}v]
Fisher 信息阵[math formula: F = E\left[\frac{\partial L}{\partial \theta}(\frac{\partial L}{\partial \theta})^T\right]]
### 2.1. 证明
把神经网络的每个权重矩阵逐列向量化，然后按一个确定的顺序排列这些向量，得到一个长向量[math formula: \theta]
对于[math formula: y = Wx + b], 令[math formula: g = \frac{\partial L}{\partial y}]
[math formula: 
\frac{\partial L}{\partial W} = \frac{\partial L}{\partial y} \cdot x^T
]
[math formula: 
\frac{\partial L}{\partial b} = \frac{\partial L}{\partial y} \cdot I
]
把 [math formula: x] 和 [math formula: I] 在后文中用[math formula: a]表示
[math formula: 
F =
\begin{matrix}
E[\frac{\partial L}{\partial \theta} \cdot \frac{\partial L}{\partial \theta}^T]
\end{matrix} =
\begin{bmatrix}
    E[\frac{\partial L}{\partial \theta_1} \cdot \frac{\partial L}{\partial \theta_1}^T]
    E[\frac{\partial L}{\partial \theta_1} \cdot \frac{\partial L}{\partial \theta_2}^T]\cdots & E[\frac{\partial L}{\partial \theta_1} \cdot \frac{\partial L}{\partial \theta_n}^T]\\
    E[\frac{\partial L}{\partial \theta_2} \cdot \frac{\partial L}{\partial \theta_1}^T]  E[\frac{\partial L}{\partial \theta_2} \cdot \frac{\partial L}{\partial \theta_2}^T]  \cdots & E[\frac{\partial L}{\partial \theta_2} \cdot \frac{\partial L}{\partial \theta_n}^T] \\
    \vdots &\vdots\\
    E[\frac{\partial L}{\partial \theta_n} \cdot \frac{\partial L}{\partial \theta_1}^T]  E[\frac{\partial L}{\partial \theta_n} \cdot \frac{\partial L}{\partial \theta_2}^T]  \cdots & E[\frac{\partial L}{\partial \theta_n} \cdot \frac{\partial L}{\partial \theta_n}^T]
\end{bmatrix}
]
[math formula: \theta_i] 表示按前述顺序排列的第 i 个权重矩阵的向量化表示。
取[math formula: F]的对角线上的矩阵作为[math formula: F]的近似。
[math formula: 
\begin{align*}
E[\frac{\partial L}{\partial \theta_i}\cdot \frac{\partial L}{\partial \theta_i}^T] &=
E[vec(g_i \cdot a_i^T)vec(g_i \cdot a_i^T)^T] \\
&= E[(a_i \otimes g_i)(a_i^T \otimes g_i^T)] \\
&= E[(a_ia_i^T)\otimes(g_ig_i^T)]\\
&\approx E[a_ia_i^T] \otimes E[g_ig_i^T] \tag{未证明}\\
&=:A_i \otimes G_i
\end{align*}
]
[math formula: 
\begin{align*}
F &\approx diag(A_1\otimes G_1, A_2 \otimes G_2, \ldots, A_n \otimes G_n) \tag{未证明}\\
&=
\begin{pmatrix}
    A_1 \otimes G_1, &0,&0,&\cdots,&0\\
    0, &A_2 \otimes G_2, &0, &\cdots, &0\\
    0, &0, &A_3 \otimes G_3, &\cdots, &0\\
    \vdots, &\vdots, &\vdots, &\ddots, &\vdots\\
    0, &0, &0, &\cdots, &A_n \otimes G_n
\end{pmatrix}
\end{align*}
]
把[math formula: A_i \otimes G_i]记作[math formula: F_i]
[math formula: A_i] 和 [math formula: G_i] 都是对称矩阵，对称矩阵的逆矩阵为对称矩阵。
[math formula: 
\begin{align*}
F_i^{-1}\cdot v_i &\approx (A_i^{-1}\otimes G_i^{-1})vec(V_i)\\
&= vec(G_i^{-1}V_i(A_i^{-1})^T)\\
&= vec(G_i^{-1}V_iA_i^{-1})\\
&= vec(E[g_i \cdot g_i^T]^{-1}V_iE[a_i \cdot a_i^T]^{-1})
\end{align*}
]
## 3. Kronecker
### 3.1. Definition
[math formula: 
A\otimes B= \begin{pmatrix}
a_{11}B & a_{12}B & \cdots & a_{1n}B \\
a_{21}B & a_{22}B & \cdots & a_{2n}B \\
\vdots & \vdots & \ddots & \vdots \\
a_{m1}B & a_{m2}B & \cdots & a_{mn}B
\end{pmatrix}
]
### 3.2. Properties
[math formula: 
vec(uv^T) = v \otimes u
]
[math formula: 
(A \otimes B)^T = (A^T \otimes B^T)
]
[math formula: 
(A \otimes B)(A^T \otimes B^T) = AA^T \otimes BB^T
]
[math formula: 
(A \otimes B)^{-1} = A^{-1} \otimes B^{-1}
]
[math formula: 
(A \otimes B)vec(X) = vec(BXA^T)
]
## 4. Useful formula
[math formula: 
(A+bc^T)^{-1} = A^{-1} + \frac{A^{-1}bc^TA^{-1}}{1-c^TA^{-1}b}
]
[math formula: 
(A+bc^T)^{-1}d= A^{-1}d + \frac{A^{-1}bc^TA^{-1}d}{1-c^TA^{-1}b}
]
[math formula: 
\begin{align*}
(F - \frac{\partial L}{\partial \theta} \frac{\partial L}{\partial \theta}^T)^{-1}d &=
F^{-1}d + \frac{F^{-1}\frac{\partial L}{\partial \theta} \frac{\partial L}{\partial \theta}^TF^{-1}d}{1-\frac{\partial L}{\partial \theta}^TF^{-1}\frac{\partial L}{\partial \theta}}\\
&= F^{-1}d + (\frac{\frac{\partial L}{\partial \theta}^TF^{-1}d}{1-\frac{\partial L}{\partial \theta}^TF^{-1}\frac{\partial L}{\partial \theta}})F^{-1}\frac{\partial L}{\partial \theta}\\
&= F^{-1}d + (\frac{\frac{\partial L}{\partial \theta}^T(F^{-1}d)}{1-\frac{\partial L}{\partial \theta}^T(F^{-1}\frac{\partial L}{\partial \theta})})(F^{-1}\frac{\partial L}{\partial \theta})\\
\end{align*}
]

93.content:
---
title: quantization
---
- model compression
- quantization
  - pytorch的quantization
    - 1. 根据权重和数据的分布（或最值）确定量化参数scale和zero\_point
      - 其中的最大值和最小值不一定是“真实”的最大值和最小值
    - 2. 量化
    - pytorch的量化误差
- PTQ and QAT
  - PTQ(Post Training Qunatization)
  - QAT(Quantization Aware Training)
    - 关于QAT中的伪量化
- paper(OneBit: Towards Extremely Low-bit Large Language Models)
  - rank-1 approximation
  - proposition 1
    - proof
  - lemma 1
    - Proof
  - proposition 2
    - proof
  - 知识蒸馏
- 参考资料
# model compression
模型压缩（轻量化）的四种常见方法
1. pruning
把权重矩阵W中的某些元素置0,希望对输出结果影响较小。
如果置0的时候按行或按列或按块，就称为结构化剪枝，否则为非结构化剪枝。
剪枝的时候可以调整权重来补偿剪枝带来的误差
2. knowledge distillation
训练一个较小的模型，用大模型（教师模型）来指导小模型（学生模型），学生模型不仅要去拟合数据，也要去逼近教师模型的分布。例如可以通过损失函数的定义来实现。例如[math formula: l = l_{1} + \alpha l_2]
3. matrix decomposition
将原来(矩阵的形状)大的权重矩阵分解成多个小的(低秩的)矩阵，用低秩矩阵近似原有权重矩阵。这样可以大大降低模型分解之后的计算量
4. 把实数上的运算转化到整数域上，加速运算和减少存储需求。
# quantization
把浮点数上的存储和运算转化成整数的存储和运算。
[math formula: [a,b]]上的运算转化到[math formula: [c,d]], 其中[math formula: [a,b]]是连续的，[math formula: [c,d]]是离散的整数。
[math formula: x_R]被量化后的指为[math formula: x_I], [math formula: x_I]被反量化后的值为[math formula: x_Q]
[math formula: x_I = round((x_R-a)(\frac{d-c}{b-a}) + c)]
[math formula: x_Q = (x_I - c)(\frac{b-a}{d-c}) + a]
把[math formula: \frac{b-a}{d-c}]记作s
[math formula: 
x_I = round((x_R-a)(\frac{1}{s}) + c) \tag{1}
]
[math formula: 
x_Q = (x_I - c)s + a \tag{2}
]
把[math formula: x_I]带入[math formula: x_Q]中
[math formula: 
x_Q = round((x_R-a)(\frac{1}{s}))s + a \tag{3}
]
如果[math formula: x_R]不在[math formula: [a,b]]内,那[math formula: x_I]也极有可能不在[c,d]内。
如果[math formula: x_I]超出了[c,d],那就取较近的边界值。
[math formula: 
  x_I = clip(round((x_R-a)(\frac{1}{s}) + c), c, d) \tag{4}
]
[math formula: 
  x_Q = (clip(round((x_R-a)(\frac{1}{s}) + c), c, d) - c)s + a \tag{5}
]
观察上述(1),(2),(3),(4)可以不依赖与区间[math formula: [a,b]]和[math formula: [c,d]]的存在。可以看作对整个数轴的线性变换。s为缩放比例。a和c决定的平移。
我们假设值都在正常范围内，没有超出[math formula: [c,d]]，不考虑clip(即使用(3)中的[math formula: x_Q])
[math formula: x_Q = x_R + \epsilon]
[math formula: 
  \begin{aligned}
  \epsilon &= x_Q - x_R\\
           &= round((x_R-a)(\frac{1}{s}))s + a - x_R\\
           &= ((x_R-a)(\frac{1}{s}) + \delta)s + a - x_R, \text{其中}\delta \sim U(-0.5,0.5) \text{均匀分布}\\
           &= s\delta
  \end{aligned}
]
所以[math formula: \epsilon \sim U(-0.5s, 0.5s)]
## pytorch的quantization
### 1. 根据权重和数据的分布（或最值）确定量化参数scale和zero_point
例如，8比特的对称量化和非对称量化
[math formula: 
\begin{aligned}
            \text{if Symmetric:}&\\
            &s = 2 \max(|x_\text{min}|, x_\text{max}) /
                \left( Q_\text{max} - Q_\text{min} \right) \\
            &z = \begin{cases}
                0 & \text{if dtype is qint8} \\
                128 & \text{otherwise}
            \end{cases}\\
            \text{Otherwise:}&\\
                &s = \left( x_\text{max} - x_\text{min}  \right ) /
                    \left( Q_\text{max} - Q_\text{min} \right ) \\
                &z = Q_\text{min} - \text{round}(x_\text{min} / s)
        \end{aligned}
]
#### 其中的最大值和最小值不一定是“真实”的最大值和最小值
确定最值有多种方式
1. 真实的最值
[math formula: 
        \begin{array}{ll}
        x_\text{min} &= \begin{cases}
            \min(X) & \text{if~}x_\text{min} = \text{None} \\
            \min\left(x_\text{min}, \min(X)\right) & \text{otherwise}
        \end{cases}\\
        x_\text{max} &= \begin{cases}
            \max(X) & \text{if~}x_\text{max} = \text{None} \\
            \max\left(x_\text{max}, \max(X)\right) & \text{otherwise}
        \end{cases}\\
        \end{array}
]
2. 最值的移动平均：
[math formula: 
        \begin{array}{ll}
                x_\text{min} = \begin{cases}
                    \min(X) & \text{if~}x_\text{min} = \text{None} \\
                    (1 - c) x_\text{min} + c \min(X) & \text{otherwise}
                \end{cases}\\
                x_\text{max} = \begin{cases}
                    \max(X) & \text{if~}x_\text{max} = \text{None} \\
                    (1 - c) x_\text{max} + c \max(X) & \text{otherwise}
                \end{cases}\\
        \end{array}
]
还有一种是根据数据的分布确定最值。（具体的怎么算得我还没看。）
### 2. 量化
[math formula: 
  x_I = round(\frac{x_R}{s}) + z
]
[math formula: 
x_Q = (x_I - z)s
]
[math formula: 
x_Q = (round(\frac{x_R}{s}))s
]
这样量化使得整数z对应实数0
### pytorch的量化误差
量化误差和之前得到的一样。
[math formula: 
  \begin{aligned}
  \epsilon &= x_Q - x_R\\
           &= round((x_R)(\frac{1}{s}))s - x_R\\
           &= ((\frac{x_R}{s}) + \delta)s - x_R, \text{其中}\delta \sim U(-0.5,0.5) \text{均匀分布}\\
           &= s\delta
  \end{aligned}
]
所以[math formula: \epsilon \sim U(-0.5s, 0.5s)]
# PTQ and QAT
PTQ和QAT都是训练完后再执行量化操作。
## PTQ(Post Training Qunatization)
直接对模型进行量化。
根据是否确定actvation的量化参数，可以分为PTDQ和PTSQ。
1. 只对权重量化，因为activation的范围是未知的（scale和zero_point未知）。等到推理的时候，计算出了activation,确定了量化参数，再对activation进行量化，称为PTDQ(D表示Dynamic)，（这个可能理解有点不准确）
2. 对权重和activation都量化，虽然不知道actvation的范围但可以根据已有的数据进行估计。称为PTSQ(S表示static)
在对模型量化前，在模型需要量化的结点插入Observer,用来统计和计算最值，从而确定量化参数。
## QAT(Quantization Aware Training)
1. 获取一个训练好的模型。
2. 插入伪量化（fake quantization）结点，在某个数据集上进行微调。（模型会在微调期间确定量化的参数）
3. 使用第2步中得到参数对模型进行量化。
QAT一般只静态量化.
### 关于QAT中的伪量化
伪量化=量化+反量化。伪量化是模型的参数仍然都是浮点数。
[math formula: 
  x_Q = (clip(round((x_R-a)(\frac{1}{s}) + c), c, d) - c)s + a
]
模型在微调时模拟了量化。伪量化结点也充当了Observer的角色。
伪量化结点的反向传播使用了（STE）
Quantization is discrete-valued, and thus the derivative is 0 almost everywhere.
[math formula: 
\frac{\partial Q(W)}{\partial W}=0
]
The neural network will learn nothing since gradients become 0 and the weights won't get updated.
[math formula: 
g_{\mathbf{W}}=\frac{\partial L}{\partial \mathbf{W}}=\frac{\partial L}{\partial Q(\mathbf{W})} \cdot \frac{\partial Q(\mathbf{W})}{\partial \mathbf{W}}=0
]
Straight-Through Estimator (STE) simply passes the gradients through the quantization as if it had been the identity function.
[math formula: 
g_{\mathbf{W}}=\frac{\partial L}{\partial \mathbf{W}}=\frac{\partial L}{\partial Q(\mathbf{W})}
]
也就是说，“将被量化”的结点后会插入一个“伪量化”结点。前向传播时，伪量化结点模拟了量化误差，使得模型在微调阶段适应量化误差；反向传播时，伪量化结点的梯度首先会被计算出来，这里STE的意思就是把对【伪量化结点的梯度】作为【将被量化的结点的梯度】
# paper(OneBit: Towards Extremely Low-bit Large Language Models)
## rank-1 approximation
奇异值分解
[math formula: A = U\Sigma V^T]
取最大的奇异值([math formula: \sigma_1])，和最大的奇异值对应的左奇异向量和右奇异向量
[math formula: \hat{A} = u_1 \sigma_1  v_1^T]
(《Foundations of Data Science》的3.5节证明了这样分解是Frobenius范数下最好的秩1分解，证明我还没看)
[math formula: W = W_{sign} \odot W_{value}] (符号和绝对值的Hadamard积)
[math formula: W_{value} = ab^T] (也就是说把矩阵的绝对值秩1分解)
[math formula: W_{value} \approx W_{sign} \odot (a b^T)]
## proposition 1
[math formula: \mathbf{X} \mathbf{W}^{\mathrm{T}} \approx\left[\left(\mathbf{X} \odot \mathbf{b}^{\mathrm{T}}\right) \mathbf{W}_{\text {sign }}^{\mathrm{T}}\right] \odot \mathbf{a}^{\mathrm{T}}]
这个命题的是为了得到想到的网络结构。这个里面的b和a就是模型架构里的g和h，其中[math formula: X\odot b^T]操作表示对[math formula: b^T]进行广播（[math formula: b^T]是一个行向量，广播的意思就是把[math formula: b^T]一行一行重复地排列下去，直到和X的形状一样。），然后和X作hadamard积。
### proof 
[math formula: w_{i j} \approx s_{i j} \cdot a_i b_j], where [math formula: s_{i j}] is the element of [math formula: \mathbf{W}_{\text {sign }}]. Hence we have
[math formula: 
\begin{aligned}
\left(\mathbf{X} \mathbf{W}^{\mathrm{T}}\right)_{i j} & = \sum_k x_{i k} w_{k j}^{\mathrm{T}}=\sum_k x_{i k} w_{j k} \\
& \approx \sum_k x_{i k} s_{j k} a_j b_k \\
& =\sum_k x_{i k} b_k s_{j k} a_j \\
& =\sum_k\left(\mathbf{X} \odot \mathbf{b}^{\mathrm{T}}\right)_{i k} s_{k j}^{\mathrm{T}} a_j \\
& =\left[\left(\mathbf{X} \odot \mathbf{b}^{\mathrm{T}}\right) \mathbf{W}_{\text {sign }}^{\mathrm{T}}\right]_{i j} a_j \\
& =\left\{\left[\left(\mathbf{X} \odot \mathbf{b}^{\mathrm{T}}\right) \mathbf{W}_{\text {sign }}^{\mathrm{T}}\right] \odot \mathbf{a}^{\mathrm{T}}\right\}_{i j} .
\end{aligned}
]
## lemma 1
Let [math formula: \sigma_i(\mathbf{W})] denote the [math formula: i]-th biggest singular value of matrix [math formula: \mathbf{W}]. The following inequality holds:
[math formula: 
\sigma_1(|\mathbf{W}|) \geq \sigma_1(\mathbf{W}) .
]
### Proof
According to the definition of induced norm, there are
[math formula: 
\begin{gathered}
\sigma_1(\mathbf{W})=\|\mathbf{W}\|_2=\max _{\mathbf{x},\|\mathbf{x}\|_2=1}\|\mathbf{W} \mathbf{x}\|_2, \\
\sigma_1(|\mathbf{W}|)=\|\mathbf{W}\|_2=\max _{\mathbf{y},\|\mathbf{y}\|_2=1}\||\mathbf{W}| \mathbf{y}\|_2 .
\end{gathered}
]
Note that for [math formula: \forall \mathbf{x},\|\mathbf{x}\|_2=1] and we have
[math formula: 
\begin{aligned}
\left\|\left|\mathbf{W}\|\mathbf{x} \mid\|_2^2\right.\right. & =\sum_i\left(\sum_j\left|w_{i j}\right|\left|x_j\right|\right)^2 \\
& \geq \sum_i\left(\left|\sum_j w_{i j} x_j\right|\right)^2 \\
& =\sum_i\left(\sum_j w_{i j} x_j\right)^2=\|\mathbf{W} \mathbf{x}\|_2^2 .
\end{aligned}
]
因为我们总是可以让y取x的绝对值，所以：
[math formula: 
\max _{\mathbf{y},\|\mathbf{y}\|_2=1}\||\mathbf{W}| \mathbf{y}\|_2 \geq \max _{\mathbf{x},\|\mathbf{x}\|_2=1}\|\mathbf{W} \mathbf{x}\|_2 .
]
This lemma is proved.
## proposition 2
Given matrices [math formula: \mathbf{W}] and [math formula: |\mathbf{W}|], [math formula: \mathbf{W}=\mathbf{W}_{\text {sign }} \odot|\mathbf{W}|]. We decompose these matrices in the way [math formula: \mathbf{W}=\mathbf{a} \mathbf{b}^{\mathrm{T}}+\mathbf{E}_1] and [math formula: |\mathbf{W}|=\tilde{\mathbf{a}} \tilde{\mathbf{b}}^{\mathrm{T}}+\mathbf{E}_2], where [math formula: \mathbf{E}_i] denotes the error matrices. In terms of the Frobenius-norm, the SVID is closer to the original matrix [math formula: \mathbf{W}] :
[math formula: 
\left\|\mathbf{W}-\mathbf{W}_{\text {sign }} \odot \tilde{\mathbf{a}} \tilde{\mathbf{b}}^T\right\|_F^2 \leq\left\|\mathbf{W}-\mathbf{a} \mathbf{b}^T\right\|_F^2 .
]
这个命题主要想说，在Frobenius范数意义下，先取绝对值再分解比直接分解的误差小。
### proof
Here we consider SVD to prove it. For SVD, the norm of the error matrix [math formula: \mathbf{E}] in the rank1 approximation is the sum of the squares of all singular values except for the largest one. We have
[math formula: E = A - \hat{A}= A - u_1 \sigma_1 v_1^T]
[math formula: \|E\|_F^2=\sum_{i, j}\left(A_{i j}-\sigma_1 u_{1 i} v_{1 j}\right)^2]
[math formula: \|E\|_F^2=\sum_{i, j} A_{i j}^2-2 \sigma_1 \sum_{i, j} A_{i j} u_{1 i} v_{1 j}+\sigma_1^2 \sum_{i, j} u_{1 i}^2 v_{1 j}^2]
u和v是U和V里的奇异向量，[math formula: u_1*\sigma_1 = Av_1], 且u和v都是单位向量。[math formula: \sum_{i, j} u_{1 i}^2 v_{1 j}^2 = (|u_1|^2\  |u_1|^2) = 1],所以
[math formula: \|E\|_F^2=\sum_{i, j} A_{i j}^2-2 \sigma_1 \sum_{i, j} A_{i j} u_{1 i} v_{1 j}+\sigma_1^2]
然后使用迹技巧
[math formula: \begin{aligned} & \sum_{i, j} A_{i j} u_{1 i} v_{1 j}=\operatorname{tr}\left(A^T u_1 v_1^T\right) \\ & =\operatorname{tr}\left(v_1^T A^T u_1\right) \\ & =\operatorname{tr}\left(u_1^T \sigma_1 u_1\right) \\ & =\sigma_1 \operatorname{tr}\left(u_1^T u_1\right) \\ & =\sigma_1\left(v_1^T u_1\right)
\\ &= \sigma_1
\end{aligned}]
所以
[math formula: \begin{aligned} & \|E\|_F^2=\sum_{i, j} A_{i j}^2-2 \sigma_1^2+\sigma_1^2 \\ & \|E\|_F^2=\sum_{i, j} A_{i j}^2-\sigma_1^2\end{aligned}]
因为矩阵的Frobenius范数的平方等于矩阵的所有奇异值的平方和，即
[math formula: \|\boldsymbol{A}\|_F=\sqrt{\sigma_1^2+\sigma_2^2+\ldots+\sigma_n^2}]
所以误差矩阵的Frobenius范数的平方等于除了最大奇异值的其他所有奇异值的平方和。
[math formula: 
\begin{gathered}
\left\|\mathbf{E}_1\right\|_F^2=\sum_{i=2}^n \sigma_i^2(\mathbf{W}), \\
\left\|\mathbf{E}_2\right\|_F^2=\sum_{i=2}^n \sigma_i^2(|\mathbf{W}|) .
\end{gathered}
]
Based on [math formula: \|\mathbf{W}\|_F^2=\||\mathbf{W}|\|_F^2], we have
[math formula: 
\sum_{i=1}^n \sigma_i^2(\mathbf{W})=\sum_{i=1}^n \sigma_i^2(|\mathbf{W}|) .
]
According to Lemma 1, we can conclude
[math formula: 
\left\|\mathbf{E}_2\right\|_F^2 \leq\left\|\mathbf{E}_1\right\|_F^2 .
]
From the equation in this proposition, we can formulate
[math formula: 
\mathbf{W}_{\text {sign }} \odot|\mathbf{W}|=\mathbf{W}_{\text {sign }} \odot \overline{\mathbf{a}} \tilde{\mathbf{b}}^{\mathrm{T}}+\mathbf{W}_{\text {sign }} \odot \mathbf{E}_{2 .}
]
Hence we have
[math formula: 
\mathbf{W}-\mathbf{W}_{\text {sign }} \odot \overline{\mathbf{a}} \tilde{\mathbf{b}}^{\mathrm{T}}=\mathbf{W}_{\text {sign }} \odot \mathbf{E}_2 .
]
Therefore
[math formula: 
\begin{aligned}
\left\|\mathbf{W}_{\text {sign }} \odot \mathbf{E}_2\right\|_F^2 & =\sum_{i, j} s_{i j}^2 e_{i j}^2=\sum_{i, j} e_{i j}^2 \\
& =\left\|\mathbf{E}_2\right\|_F^2 \leq\left\|\mathbf{E}_1\right\|_F^2,
\end{aligned}
]
where [math formula: s_{i j}= \pm 1] is the element of [math formula: \mathbf{W}_{\text {sign }}]. Hence the inequation in this proposition is proved.
## 知识蒸馏
这里就是用命题1得到的Y的表达式。
[math formula: \begin{array}{r}\mathbf{W}_{ \pm 1}=\operatorname{Sign}(\mathbf{W}), \\ \mathbf{Y}=\left[(\mathbf{X} \odot \mathbf{g}) \mathbf{W}_{ \pm 1}^{\mathrm{T}}\right] \odot \mathbf{h}, \\ \mathbf{Z}=\text { LayerNorm }(\mathbf{Y}),\end{array}]
[math formula: 
\mathcal{L}_{\mathrm{CE}}=-\frac{1}{n_s} \sum_{i=1}^{n_s} \sum_c P_c^{\mathcal{T}}\left(\mathbf{o}_i\right) \log P_c^{\mathcal{S}}\left(\mathbf{o}_i\right),
]
where [math formula: c] denotes the number of classes and [math formula: n_s] denotes the number of training samples in the current batch. [math formula: \mathcal{T}] and [math formula: \mathcal{S}] are the teacher model and student model, respectively. The error of hidden states is defined as
[math formula: 
\mathcal{L}_{\text {MSE }}=\sum_{i=1}^{n_s} \sum_{j=1}^{n_l}\left\|\frac{\mathbf{q}_{i, j}^{\mathcal{T}}}{\left\|\mathbf{q}_{i, j}^{\mathcal{T}}\right\|_2}-\frac{\mathbf{q}_{i, j}^{\mathcal{S}}}{\left\|\mathbf{q}_{i, j}^{\mathcal{S}}\right\|_2}\right\|_2^2,
]
where [math formula: n_l] denotes the number of layers and [math formula: \mathbf{q}] denotes the hidden state. Hence the final objective function can be formulated as
[math formula: 
\mathcal{L}_{\mathrm{KD}}=\mathcal{L}_{\mathrm{CE}}+\alpha \mathcal{L}_{\mathrm{MSE}},
]
[math formula: L_{CE}]使得学生模型在输出上接近教师模型。
[math formula: L_{MSE}]使得学生模型在内部参数上接近教师模型。
# 参考资料
1. https://leimao.github.io/article/Neural-Networks-Quantization/#Quantization
2. https://zhuanlan.zhihu.com/p/548174416
3. https://www.dropbox.com/scl/fi/1mo0umu0qtq7uxap2l5m3/lec06.pdf?rlkey=bdl2mgusgajddjuvjxb0fot36&dl=0
4. https://zhuanlan.zhihu.com/p/645259854
5. 论文：OneBit: Towards Extremely Low-bit Large Language Models
6. 论文：Quantization and Training of Neural Networks for Efficient Integer-Arithmetic-Only Inference
7. 论文：A Survey on Model Compression for Large Language Models
